summary: Deep map of every AI roster-management subsystem + competing FM-depth architectures; design a complete overhaul: correct role-filling, strategic (short+long-term) squad planning, starting/benching, coach archetypes, and a tier-2/solo-Q scouting pipeline that signs only on genuine need

plan.summary: All load-bearing claims verified against source: auto_fill_roster's soft-role-filter bug is real (Team.cpp:939-943 keeps the unfiltered `valid` pool when no on-role FA exists, then signs best-of-any-role); swap_roster_slots (732) validates opposite-line-side, iter_swaps, and re-enforces IGL/Flex; release_player (714) also re-enforces; scout_top_fas (765) and ai_manage_roster (1511) are confirmed DEAD (only defs + comment mentions at GameManager.cpp:910,2140,2183, no live callers); ai_full_offseason_pass (1631) Stage layout matches; sign_player does NOT debit budget (caller must). CHOSEN: a hybrid that takes SCORING-FUNCTION-FIRST's single-unified-scorer discipline (lowest risk of the four divergent formulas drifting) and STAGED-PIPELINE's clean strategy->scout->act separation, but DROPS the persistent shortlist_ member (stale-PlayerPtr footgun) and the squad_plan member in favor of TRANSIENT per-pass gap/shortlist vectors — equally correct, no new mutable team state to invalidate. The whole overhaul is one new pure scorer (upgrade_score / evaluate_signing) + one transient gap vector + one strategy_profile table, consumed by a rewritten auto_fill_roster, a rewritten ai_full_offseason_pass (kept as the unchanged-signature entrypoint), a new optimize_starting_five, and a new GameManager scout-source merger. Every mutation continues to route through sign_player/release_player/resign_player/swap_roster_slots so never-free + IGL/Flex + roster-cap-7 hold for free. Call sites at GameManager.cpp:2284 and 4875 keep their (free_agents, year+1, log) shape untouched. Phases are ordered so each compiles and smoke-tests green standalone: foundation scorer+gap (behavior-neutral) -> need-gated role-fill -> strategy_profile gating -> offseason rebuild orchestration -> merit start-5 + benching -> coach archetypes -> mid-season + scouting pipeline -> lower-tier differentiation. Two test bands will shift and are flagged (mid-season user-team gating, and league-wide roster composition).
plan.chosen_architecture: Hybrid: SCORING-FUNCTION-FIRST core (one unified pure scorer `evaluate_signing` + one `strategy_profile` table + a transient RoleGap vector as the single need model) wired through STAGED-PIPELINE's strategy->scout->act separation, but using TRANSIENT per-pass shortlist/gap vectors instead of a persistent squad_plan/shortlist_ member (avoids stale-PlayerPtr invalidation and new serializable team state). ai_full_offseason_pass stays as the unchanged-signature entrypoint and becomes a thin driver; auto_fill_roster is rewritten to consume the same gap+scorer; optimize_starting_five is the only new mutator and routes exclusively through swap_roster_slots. scout_top_fas + ai_manage_roster deleted after harvesting their need-diff skeleton.
data_model_changes: NEW (transient, no storage) struct RoleGap { std::array<int,Role::Count> required{}; std::array<int,Role::Count> have{}; Role flex_demand; int gap(Role) const; } returned by-value from new Team::compute_roster_gap() const — decoupled from target_comp/CompPlan so build_round_selection's per-map plan.need keeps its doubled-bucket meaning. Pure addition, nothing reads it until the new fill loop does.

NEW POD struct StrategyProfile { double ovr_w, pot_w, youth_w, timeline_w, value_w, risk_sign; int max_vet_age, min_pot, contract_len_delta, max_buys, bench_buys; double upgrade_margin; bool prefer_develop, sell_developed; } + free fn strategy_profile(Team::Strategy, const Coach*) in Team.cpp. Default-construct yields neutral (Bridge-like) weights so any un-migrated call site is safe. The 5 existing strategy switch-sites migrate to read it one at a time (behavior-preserving per edit).

NEW POD struct SignCtx { const Team* team; Role slot; const Player* incumbent; const RoleGap* gap; long long role_budget_k; const StrategyProfile* prof; const Coach* coach; } + free fn double evaluate_signing(const SignCtx&, const Player& cand) (a.k.a. upgrade_score) — THE unified pure/O(1) scorer. Multiplicative on need x role_fit x (candidate-incumbent) delta x affordability x strategy/coach lean. Folds in existing timeline_fit_score, role_fit_score (currently acquisition-dead), market_value_estimate value-for-money, and solo_mmr/peak_mmr (currently unused in scoring).

NEW free fn double starter_value_now(const Player&) hoisted byte-equivalent out of the Team.cpp:1711 lambda (ovr + age<=24:(pot-ovr)*0.30 + form*8 - age>=30:5) to file scope so ai_full_offseason_pass Stage 2, optimize_starting_five, and run_mid_season_replacements share one 'how good right now' scorer.

NEW methods: Team::compute_roster_gap() const, Team::reserve_role_budgets(const RoleGap&) -> std::array<long long,Role::Count>, Team::optimize_starting_five(int current_year). NEW GameManager helper std::vector<PlayerPtr> gather_scout_sources(const std::string& region) (or build_scout_shortlist) merging FA ladder slice + top-N solo-Q-by-MMR FAs + tier-2 risers (flagged poach). No new mutable member state on Team or Player.

NO new Player fields for v1: solo_mmr/peak_mmr (Player.h:516-517) and role_fit_score (Player.cpp:161) already exist and are merely newly READ by the scorer. (Optional future: a recent-form ring buffer; until then reuse avg_match_rating().) No serialization break.

DELETE Team::scout_top_fas (Team.cpp:765 + Team.h:363) and Team::ai_manage_roster (Team.cpp:1511 + Team.h:395) after harvesting the need-diff skeleton; scrub the 3 stale comment mentions (GameManager.cpp:910,2183; Team.h:176,419,430). Verified no live callers.

phases.title: Foundation: unified scorer + transient gap vector + strategy_profile table (behavior-neutral, nothing wired yet)

phases.where: Team.cpp anon namespace: add struct StrategyProfile + strategy_profile(Strategy, const Coach*) (initially returning weights byte-equivalent to today's 5 scattered switches), struct SignCtx, evaluate_signing(SignCtx, Player), free starter_value_now(Player); Team.h + Team.cpp: struct RoleGap + Team::compute_roster_gap() const + Team::reserve_role_budgets(). Migrate ONLY CandidateRanker::strat_score (Team.cpp:831) and the Stage-2 lambda (1711) to delegate to the new fns as the first behavior-preserving migration.
phases.detail: Pure additive code. strategy_profile encodes the existing Contender/WinNow (ovr-heavy, low margin, more buys) vs Rebuilding/DevFocus/TalentFarm (pot/youth-heavy, high vet-age penalty, fewer buys) grouping that already exists in 5 places, plus neutral Bridge/BudgetRoster. evaluate_signing = need_magnitude(gap) * role_fit_score(slot) * delta_blend(candidate vs incumbent via starter_value_now) * strategy/coach lean * affordability(role_budget); coach param accepted but returns 1.0 until Phase 6. compute_roster_gap revives the scout_top_fas need-diff: have[role] from primary_role, required = canonical 1/1/1/1/1 + flex_demand = thinnest core role by role_fit coverage (NOT strength-doubled). Keep starter_value lambda call site replaced by the free fn (byte-equivalent).

phases.detail: Replace the literal D/C/I/S template (1071-1075) + soft role filter (939-943). New loop: compute gap, reserve per-role budget envelope BEFORE any debit (fixes greedy starvation into the solo_mmr fallback at 1044), then for each gap largest-first build candidate pool and pick argmax evaluate_signing; sign via sign_player+decide_contract_years only if score>epsilon AND desire_accepts AND within role envelope; otherwise LEAVE the slot open (next year / pathological backstop) — never silently sign off-role. role_fit lets a strong flex (>=~0.82) fill a hole; a random off-role duelist scores low and cannot. Keep the import gate but make it deterministic-on-need rather than a pure coin flip (still respect max_imports). Keep enforce_one_igl/flex at the end.

Assert full sim-year run still ends every tier-1 team at 5 starters (no team stuck <5). Re-run the existing role-correctness / roster-fill test band.

Mitigate: keep the pathological while-loop backstop (Team.cpp:1093) so a vacancy is always eventually filled, and assert '5 starters across many sim-years' in the smoke test. architectures.angle: SQUAD-PLANNER (FM-style): every team owns a persistent SquadPlan (a per-role target depth + strategy-weighted age/quality targets, reweighted by coach archetype). A single shared engine — run for EVERY team at offseason AND on the monthly mid-season tick — computes the gap vector (plan vs current roster by primary_role/role_fit), builds ONE ranked, need-gated shortlist over ALL sources (FA slice of the regional ladder + tier-2 risers + top solo-Q FAs by MMR), executes the best AFFORDABLE moves within a reserved per-role budget envelope, then elects a merit-based starting 5 via swap_roster_slots and benches the rest. The plan (not roster-trailing labels) drives construction; the same scorer ranks every candidate everywhere.
architecture: LAYER 0 - strategy_profile(Strategy, const Coach*): ONE free function in Team.cpp returning a StrategyProfile POD {double ovr_w, pot_w, youth_w, timeline_w, value_w; int max_vet_age, min_pot_floor; double upgrade_margin; int max_buys, bench_buys; bool prefer_develop, sell_developed;}. Replaces the 5 duplicated strategy switches (CandidateRanker::strat_score, risk_bias, snake score_candidate, decide_contract_years, team_eval overlay). Coach archetype reweights it: DevelopmentCoach/VeteranMentor/BudgetBalancer push youth_w/prefer_develop and lower max_buys/import; Pragmatist/AggressiveRiskTaker push ovr_w/lower upgrade_margin/raise max_buys. Null-coach -> neutral. This is the SINGLE table; coach influence enters here so all sites inherit it.

LAYER 1 - SquadPlan (new member on Team, persistent): desired_depth[Role::Count] (canonical 1/1/1/1/1 + one strategy/coach-chosen 2nd-of-role for the flex slot, replacing pick_best_comp_for_roster's strength-bias with a REAL vacancy model) plus target_age_band and target_quality derived from strategy_profile. Recomputed at the START of each pass (offseason + mid-season) by Team::rebuild_squad_plan(coach) BEFORE any signing - never as a trailing label.

LAYER 2 - compute_gaps(): Team method producing std::vector<RoleGap>{Role role; int shortfall; double incumbent_value;}. shortfall = desired_depth[role] - count_by_primary_role(role); incumbent_value = best starter_value currently covering that role (via role_fit_score so a flex can partially cover). Severity-ordered: have==0 core holes first, then largest shortfall, ties broken by weakest role_fit coverage. This is the need vector that GATES every signing.

LAYER 3 - evaluate_signing(team, player, Role slot, const Player* incumbent, StrategyProfile): THE single unified scorer all paths route through. = need_magnitude(gap)*role_fit_score(player,slot) + profile.ovr_w*ovr + profile.pot_w*potential + profile.youth_w*youth + profile.timeline_w*timeline_fit_score + profile.value_w*(market_value_estimate - ask) + solo_mmr signal + chemistry. Crucially scores (candidate - incumbent_at_slot), so it is upgrade-over-incumbent EVERYWHERE, not just Stage-2. Pure/O(1) for the snake-draft inner loop. CandidateRanker is kept but becomes a thin caller of this.

LAYER 4 - ScoutPipeline (GameManager helper build_scout_shortlist(team, region)): unions three sources into one vector of ScoutTarget{PlayerPtr p; SourceKind kind; TeamPtr current_club;}: (a) FA slice of solo_qs[region]->global_ladder(); (b) tier-2 risers walked from tier_leagues_[region][>=2] rosters passing an avg_match_rating/standing bar; (c) top-N FAs by solo_mmr/peak_mmr (the solo-Q phenom signal, currently unused in scoring). Each target carries its acquisition path so the executor knows FA-sign vs release-then-sign poach.

LAYER 5 - execute_plan(team, shortlist, gaps, profile, budget_env, log): the decide-and-act loop. Reserve a per-role budget envelope across all open gaps FIRST (fixes greedy debit starving later roles into the solo_mmr fallback). For each gap in severity order, rank shortlist by evaluate_signing for THAT slot, take the best affordable candidate that clears profile.upgrade_margin over incumbent AND desire_accepts; acquire via the right path. Honors max_buys (strategy/coach), max_imports, roster cap 7, never silently signs off-role (role_fit penalty, not hard-drop, but gap stays open if nothing clears the bar).

LAYER 6 - optimize_starting_five(current_year): the merit benching pass. Snapshot roster, for each bench player (roster[5..]) vs weakest same-role-or-flex-coverable starter compute starter_value delta; collect swaps clearing a +3..4 hysteresis margin (prospect-protect age<=21&pot>=75, sample gate season_matches>=6), apply via swap_roster_slots ONLY (free IGL/Flex re-election, still-paid bench). Batch-compute then apply to avoid mid-loop index churn.

LAYER 7 - ONE entrypoint Team::ai_roster_review(sources, current_year, PassKind kind, log): rebuild_squad_plan -> compute_gaps -> execute_plan -> (TalentFarm sell pass) -> optimize_starting_five. kind=Offseason does full buys+bench-depth; kind=MidSeason caps moves (1-2) and prefers internal swap before any cut. ai_full_offseason_pass becomes a thin wrapper calling this with Offseason. run_mid_season_replacements calls it with MidSeason. scout_top_fas + ai_manage_roster DELETED (their need-diff + drop+refill skeletons folded in).

WIRING: GameManager::run_end_of_year (2284) and run_lower_tier_offseason (4875) call ai_roster_review(Offseason) - unchanged call shape, just pass the unioned scout sources instead of bare fa_pool. advance_day monthly tick (1045-1052) routes run_mid_season_replacements through ai_roster_review(MidSeason), now gated on (t!=user_team || user_auto_manage). Lower tiers get tier-differentiated strategy assignment (break BudgetRoster monoculture) since they share the same engine.
data_model: NEW struct StrategyProfile (POD in Team.cpp anon namespace or Team.h): {double ovr_w, pot_w, youth_w, timeline_w, value_w, risk_sign; int max_vet_age, min_pot_floor, contract_len_delta; double upgrade_margin; int max_buys, bench_buys; bool prefer_develop, sell_developed;}. KEEP-COMPILING: pure new type, no existing code references it until the 5 switch sites are migrated one at a time; old switches can coexist during migration.

NEW member Team::squad_plan{} (Team.h:254 next to target_comp). decision_flow: STEP 1 (plan): Team::rebuild_squad_plan(head_coach.get()) - profile = strategy_profile(strategy, head_coach.get()); set squad_plan.desired_depth = canonical 1/1/1/1/1, then add the +1 'flex/2nd' to the role the roster can LEAST cover (thinnest by role_fit), NOT the role it's strongest in. develop_mode + target_age_band from profile. Runs FIRST, before any candidate is looked at, at both offseason and mid-season.

STEP 2 (gaps): gaps = compute_gaps() - have[role] from primary_role counts; shortfall = desired - have; incumbent_value = best current coverage via starter_value/role_fit. Severity-sort: have==0 core holes -> largest shortfall -> weakest flex coverage. This produces 'I need a Sentinel because I have zero' which the old strength-doubled model literally could not express.

STEP 3 (shortlist): sources = GameManager::build_scout_shortlist(team, region): FA ladder slice + tier-2 risers (release-then-sign poach path) + top-N solo_mmr FAs. Each ScoutTarget tagged with acquisition path. Built once per pass per team; passed into the engine so all three talent streams compete in ONE ranking.

STEP 4 (reserve budget): partition affordable budget into a per-role envelope across the open gaps (severity-weighted) BEFORE signing, so an early Duelist buy cannot starve the Sentinel slot into the random solo_mmr fallback. max_buys (from profile/coach) caps how many gaps we'll actually fund this pass; mid-season caps tighter (1-2).

STEP 5 (decide+act per gap, severity order): for the gap's role, rank shortlist by evaluate_signing(team, target.p, gap.role, incumbent_at_slot, profile). Take best candidate that (a) is affordable within the role envelope, (b) clears profile.upgrade_margin over incumbent_value, (c) passes strategy GATES (Rebuilder hard-skips age>=max_vet_age non-stars; Contender hard-skips low-pot benchwarmers), (d) desire_accepts. role_fit lets a strong flex fill a hole but off-role is penalized, never silent - if nothing clears the bar the gap stays open (no wrong-role sign).

STEP 6 (acquire by path): FA -> sign_player(p, decide_contract_years, year) + manual budget debit. tier-2 riser/walked star -> current_club->release_player(p) THEN team->sign_player + debit + sync_player_ranked_region if cross-region (never-free preserved, mirrors B8). Decrement role envelope + max_buys. Loop to next gap.

STEP 7 (develop vs buy fork): if profile.prefer_develop (DevelopmentCoach/Rebuilder/TalentFarm) and an internal young high-pot player already covers the gap acceptably, KEEP+coach instead of buying - only buy if a genuine youth upgrade exists. TalentFarm sell_developed: release a prospect whose pot-ovr gap has closed for budget (the never-implemented sell action).

STEP 8 (bench-depth, offseason only): if exactly 5 starters and profile.bench_buys>0 and budget allows, sign ONE depth piece scored by evaluate_signing with bench weighting (Contender->proven insurance, TalentFarm->cheap high-pot flip), skipping roles already stacked 2+. Lower-tier bench gate decoupled from the 400K floor via tier flag.

STEP 9 (merit start-5): optimize_starting_five(year) - snapshot, for each bench reserve vs weakest same-role/flex-coverable starter compute starter_value delta, collect swaps clearing +3..4 hysteresis (prospect-protect, season_matches>=6 gate), apply ALL via swap_roster_slots only. IGL/Flex legality auto-maintained. Runs at BOTH offseason (end of review) and mid-season tick.

STEP 10 (mid-season specifics): on the 30-day tick, ai_roster_review(MidSeason) gated on user_auto_manage; recompute plan+gaps fresh (no stale tag), prefer an internal swap_roster_slots over any cut (stops releasing depth it could bench), allow at most 1-2 need-gated signings of a hot tier-2 riser / top-solo-Q FA that fills a real hole, emit push_news 'Roster Move' with distinct BENCH/SIGN/PROMOTE headlines.
pros: One unified evaluate_signing + one strategy_profile table collapse FOUR divergent scorers and FIVE duplicated strategy switches into single chokepoints - every fix (need-magnitude, upgrade-over-incumbent, value-for-money, coach influence) lands everywhere at once, which is the largest depth win available.

Plan-DRIVES-construction (gaps computed BEFORE signing) structurally fixes the #1 'signs a 2nd Duelist when it needs a Sentinel' bug: the system can finally express have==0 core holes and severity-order them ahead of strength-doubled surplus.

Delivers all 5 required outcomes through ONE engine shared by offseason + mid-season + tier-1 + tier-2: correct role-filling (gap-gated role_fit), strategy (profile gates not nudges), merit benching (optimize_starting_five via the existing safe mutator), coach archetypes (reweight the single profile), scouting pipeline (3-source shortlist, need-gated).

Never-free + IGL/Flex invariants preserved for free: all mutation routes through the verified sign_player/release_player/swap_roster_slots which already re-run enforce_one_igl/flex; cross-tier poach mirrors the proven B8 release-then-sign + sync_player_ranked_region pattern.

Keeps every existing call site compiling: ai_full_offseason_pass kept as a thin wrapper; target_comp and the in-match comp plumbing untouched (squad_plan is a decoupled parallel vector); new types are additive PODs migrated incrementally.

Per-role reserved budget envelope eliminates the greedy-debit starvation that dumps the last role into the random solo_mmr fallback - coordinated multi-move planning instead of a per-slot greedy loop.
Revives genuinely useful dead logic (need-diff skeleton, scout prefs as flavour) rather than maintaining two parallel unused implementations; deletes the confusion.
cons: Large surface: replacing auto_fill_roster + ai_full_offseason_pass, adding 6 new structs/methods, migrating 5 strategy switch sites, and rewiring 3 GameManager call sites is a big single change - needs careful incremental migration (old switches coexisting) to keep the build green throughout.

squad_plan vs target_comp duality is a deliberate decoupling but adds a second need-representation; risk of future confusion about which drives what (acquisition vs per-map Flex) - must be documented at both member declarations.

evaluate_signing must stay pure/O(1) for the snake-draft inner loop over the full ladder; folding timeline_fit + role_fit + mmr + chemistry into it risks a perf regression on the hottest path - needs the table-lookup discipline timeline_fit_score already uses, and benchmarking.

Mid-season now mutates every AI team monthly (3 sources x gap loop x optimize_starting_five) - heavier than today's single-worst-starter scan; could shift sim performance and league churn, needs hysteresis + max_buys caps tuned to avoid thrash and tier-2 talent drain.

Tier-2 poaching + cross-region sign introduces new league-balance dynamics (talent vacuum, circular promotion) that the current team-granular promotion never had - requires per-team poach caps and validation that run_lower_tier_offseason backfills believably.

Gating mid-season on user_auto_manage CHANGES current behavior (today run_mid_season_replacements already fires on the user team unconditionally) - correct per the spec but a behavior change existing saves/tests may assume.

Coach roster_prefs + strategy reweighting interacts with the existing strategy inertia (previous_strategy) and Window axis; must feed the SUGGESTED strategy (not bypass inertia) and reconcile Window/Strategy contradictions or behavior stays incoherent at the edges.

architectures.angle: SCORING-FUNCTION-FIRST (minimal additive): one need-gated upgrade score, reused by offseason fill, mid-season review, and starting-5 election. Replace the two rewrite-surface functions (auto_fill_roster, ai_full_offseason_pass) plus retarget the existing call sites; revive the dead need-diff skeleton from scout_top_fas instead of the strength-doubled comp tag. The thesis: role-correctness, strategy, benching, and scouting are all the SAME bug — every signing path scores absolute value, never need-gated value-over-incumbent. Fix the score once; correctness propagates everywhere it is consumed.
architecture: ONE scorer is the whole design. Add a free function in Team.cpp's anon namespace: double upgrade_score(const SignCtx& ctx, const Player& cand) returning the product role_need x role_fit x ovr_delta x strategy_fit x affordability x coach_lean. It is pure, O(1), and the SINGLE authority for every acquire/bench/scout decision. SignCtx is a small POD bundling the inputs each factor reads (team ref, target Role, incumbent ptr-or-null, RoleGap vector, budget envelope, Strategy, head_coach ptr). Multiplicative not additive: any zero factor (no genuine need, unaffordable, off-window vet for a rebuilder) drives the score to ~0 so the move simply does not happen — this is what turns strategy from a score-nudge into a GATE without adding hard if-branches everywhere.

FACTOR role_need: built from a NEW roster_gap_vector computed at the START of the pass (not the trailing refresh_target_comp at 1104). Revive the scout_top_fas need-diff skeleton (Team.cpp:765-772): required = canonical D/C/I/S + one demand-driven flex; have[role] from roster primary_role; gap = max(0, required-have). role_need = saturating f(gap): empty core role (have==0) ~1.0, surplus/doubled role ~0.0. This is the #1 'signs a 2nd Duelist when it needs a Sentinel' fix — a role you already have scores ~0 regardless of how good the candidate is.

FACTOR role_fit: reuse the EXISTING but currently acquisition-dead Player::role_fit_score(Role) (Player.cpp:161) = 1.0 on primary, weighted blend off-role. This replaces the soft hard-filter bug at fill_one (Team.cpp:939-943): instead of silently signing an off-role player when no on-role FA exists, a strong flex scores ~0.82 and CAN fill the hole, a random off-role duelist scores low and cannot. role_fit x role_need together make 'fills wrong roles' structurally impossible — you can only score high on a role you both NEED and can COVER.

FACTOR strategy_fit + coach_lean via ONE shared table strategy_profile(Strategy) and coach_profile(CoachPersonality) returning weights {ovr_w, pot_w, youth_w, max_vet_age, min_pot, timeline_w, risk_sign, contract_len_delta}. Collapses the 5 duplicated strategy switch-sites (CandidateRanker.strat_score 831, risk_bias 980, snake 3014, decide_contract_years 1573, team_eval 1863) into one source. strategy_fit folds in timeline_fit_score (Team.cpp:2235) at timeline_w so Contender~0 weight / Rebuilder~high. coach_lean nudges the same axes (DevelopmentCoach raises youth_w, Pragmatist raises ovr_w) feeding the suggested strategy, respecting existing inertia. ovr_delta = (cand.ovr - incumbent.ovr) blended with starter_value's youth+form terms (Team.cpp:1711) so it works for both fill (incumbent=null -> absolute) and upgrade (incumbent=current starter at that role).

THREE thin consumers, ONE entrypoint. Rewrite auto_fill_roster: for each gapped role (largest gap first), call upgrade_score over the FA pool, sign the argmax via sign_player+decide_contract_years; reserve a per-role budget envelope BEFORE signing so an early Duelist can't starve the Sentinel into the solo_mmr fallback (1044). Rewrite ai_full_offseason_pass to call: (a) compute_roster_gap -> (b) fill gaps -> (c) optimize_starting_five (the bench pass) -> (d) scout poach pass. Lift kMaxUpgradesPerPass(1724)/bench-cap(1822) into a strategy-driven moves_budget from strategy_profile (WinNow buys more). Keep run_end_of_year(2284) + run_lower_tier_offseason(4875) calling the same (free_agents, year+1, log) signature unchanged.

NEW Team::optimize_starting_five(int year): the merit benching pass, mutating ONLY through the existing swap_roster_slots (Team.cpp:732, which already re-runs enforce_one_igl/flex and keeps benchee paid). Snapshot first, then for each bench reserve vs weakest same-role starter compute upgrade_score with incumbent=starter; swap when delta clears a +3-4 hysteresis margin; protect young high-pot starters (age<=21 && pot>=75); require season_matches>=6 form sample. Wire into BOTH offseason (inside the pass, before report) and mid-season (run_mid_season_replacements GameManager.cpp:~1256, preferring an internal bench over an external cut). Gate user team on user_auto_manage.

SCOUTING pipeline as a need-gated source widening, NOT a new scorer. Build candidate pool = FAs (team_name=='Free Agent', add solo_mmr/peak_mmr as a fresh-signal input since FA avg_match_rating is stale) UNION top tier-2 risers (walk tier_leagues_[region] tiers>=2, bar on avg_match_rating + Team::wins) flagged as POACH targets. Every candidate runs the SAME upgrade_score, so a riser/solo-Q phenom is signed ONLY when role_need>0 and ovr_delta>0 (fills a hole AND upgrades). Poach path mirrors B8 (GameManager.cpp:2131): source_team->release_player -> tier1->sign_player -> debit budget -> sync_player_ranked_region if cross-region. Cap one poach/team/offseason. Never-free preserved: release+sign only, ladder membership untouched.
data_model: struct RoleGap { std::array<int,Role::Count> required{}; std::array<int,Role::Count> have{}; int gap(Role r) const; Role flex_demand; }; — the acquisition need, DECOUPLED from target_comp/CompPlan so build_round_selection's per-map plan.need (Team.cpp:1345) keeps its 5-role doubled-bucket meaning. Computed by new Team::compute_roster_gap() const at the START of each pass. Keep-compiling: pure addition, nothing reads it yet until the new fill loop does.

struct SignCtx { const Team* team; Role slot; const Player* incumbent; const RoleGap* gap; long long role_budget_k; Strategy strat; const Coach* coach; }; — the bundle passed to upgrade_score. POD, header-only, no behavior. Keep-compiling: new struct, zero existing call sites touched.

struct StrategyProfile { double ovr_w, pot_w, youth_w, timeline_w; int max_vet_age, min_pot, contract_len_delta; double risk_sign; }; + free StrategyProfile strategy_profile(Strategy) and coach_lean(const Coach*). Keep-compiling: the 5 existing strategy switch-sites are REFACTORED to read this table one at a time — each refactor is behavior-preserving on its own, so the build stays green between edits. CandidateRanker (Team.cpp:826) is KEPT as infra but its total() is reimplemented to delegate to upgrade_score with incumbent=null.

Player: NO new fields needed for v1 — solo_mmr/peak_mmr (Player.h:516-517) and role_fit_score (Player.cpp:161) already exist; just newly READ by the scorer. Optional later: a small recent-form ring buffer alongside season_rating_total (Player.h:482) for a rolling last-N form term in ovr_delta; until then reuse avg_match_rating(). Keep-compiling: additive, defaulted, no serialization break.

Team: add decls compute_roster_gap() const, optimize_starting_five(int), and reserve_role_budgets(const RoleGap&) -> per-role envelope. DELETE the two dead functions (scout_top_fas Team.cpp:765 + Team.h:363, ai_manage_roster Team.cpp:1511 + Team.h:395) after harvesting the need-diff skeleton, so there is ONE authoritative path. Keep-compiling: confirm via Grep they are referenced only in comments (GameManager.cpp:910,2183) before removal; remove decl+def+comments together in one edit.
decision_flow: 1. compute_roster_gap(): have[role] from roster primary_role; required = D/C/I/S each 1 + flex_demand = thinnest core role by role_fit coverage (NOT the strength-doubled pick_best_comp_for_roster). Emit RoleGap. This runs FIRST, replacing the trailing refresh_target_comp(1104) as the driver.

2. reserve_role_budgets(gap): split affordable budget across the positive gaps (weighted by strategy_profile so a WinNow over-allocates to its weakest core role) so no greedy early signing starves a later role into the solo_mmr fallback (Team.cpp:1044-1050).

3. FILL loop, largest gap first: build candidate pool (regional FAs + import-gated imports + need-gated tier-2 risers + top solo-Q FAs by solo_mmr). For each candidate compute upgrade_score(SignCtx{slot=gapRole, incumbent=null, role_budget}). If argmax score > epsilon AND desire_accepts: sign_player + decide_contract_years(year+1). role_need x role_fit guarantee right-role; multiplicative affordability respects the envelope. Skip the slot (leave for next year) rather than sign off-role — kills the soft-filter bug.

4. UPGRADE pass (replaces Stage 2): for each filled role, incumbent = current starter at that role (keyed by primary_role, NOT roster[0..4] index which is unstable). Score best same-role/flex-cover candidate with incumbent set; if upgrade_score delta clears the strategy-scaled threshold and within moves_budget, cut-and-replace via release_player+sign_player. moves_budget from strategy_profile (WinNow many, Rebuilder few/youth-only).

5. optimize_starting_five(year): snapshot roster; for each bench reserve vs weakest same-role starter, upgrade_score(incumbent=starter); collect swaps clearing +3-4 hysteresis, skipping young-prospect starters and sub-6-match samples; apply via swap_roster_slots (auto re-runs enforce_one_igl/flex, benchee stays paid). This is the merit benching the AI never did.

6. SCOUT poach commit: for the single best need-gated tier-2 riser / solo-Q FA whose upgrade_score beats the worst startable incumbent, run the never-free poach: source->release_player -> this->sign_player -> debit budget -> sync_player_ranked_region if cross-region. Cap 1 poach/team/offseason to avoid draining tier-2.

7. CLOSE: enforce_one_igl + enforce_one_flex (already inside every sign/release/swap, so invariant holds for free); refresh_target_comp LAST only as a trailing per-map LABEL (its real consumer build_round_selection), never as the acquisition driver. Mid-season: same compute_roster_gap + optimize_starting_five + need-gated FA/riser check on the 30-day tick (GameManager.cpp:1045), gated user_auto_manage.
pros: Smallest surface that fixes all five asks at once: one scorer + one gap vector + one strategy table, consumed by fill/upgrade/bench/scout. Role-correctness, strategy, benching, scouting are literally the same fix (need-gated value-over-incumbent), so they cannot drift apart the way the 4 divergent formulas + 2 need definitions do today.

Reuses existing infra rather than rebuilding: role_fit_score (Player.cpp:161, dead for acquisition) wired in; timeline_fit_score (2235) becomes the strategy_fit spine; CandidateRanker (826) kept as a thin delegate; swap_roster_slots (732) is the benching mutator unchanged; B8 (2131) is the poach template. Less new code, less risk.

Multiplicative gating makes strategy/coach actually GATE (a rebuilder's score for a 30yo vet collapses to ~0) instead of nudging a blended score by 0.35 weight — directly answers 'no short/long-term thinking' and 'coach archetypes ignored' without scattering hard if-branches.

Never-free + IGL/Flex invariants preserved automatically because EVERY mutation still routes through sign_player/release_player/resign_player/swap_roster_slots, all of which re-enforce. Call-site signatures (year+1, log) unchanged, so run_end_of_year/run_lower_tier_offseason keep compiling.
Per-role budget envelope + largest-gap-first ordering kills the greedy-debit starvation that drops the Sentinel into the random solo_mmr fallback — a quiet but real quality win.

Tier-2 risers + top solo-Q FAs enter through the SAME need-gated scorer, so the pipeline is correct-by-construction: a riser is signed only when he fills a hole AND upgrades, never as undifferentiated noise.
cons: A single multiplicative scorer is sensitive to factor calibration: if any factor is mis-scaled to ~0 too eagerly, teams under-sign and sit below 5 (caught by the pathological backstop, but it degrades quality). Needs a tuning/smoke-test pass asserting full rosters + correct roles across many sim-years.

The flex_demand / 'thinnest role by role_fit coverage' computation is the one genuinely new piece of judgement; get it wrong and you reintroduce a soft strength-bias. Mitigated by anchoring on have==0 core roles first, but it's the riskiest factor.

Decoupling roster_gap_vector from target_comp/CompPlan adds a second need representation; must keep them from contradicting (acquisition gap vs per-map plan.need). Documented split, but a maintenance footgun if a future edit conflates them.

Cross-tier poach (release tier-2 -> sign tier-1 -> manual budget debit -> sync region) is new never-free plumbing with no existing helper; easy to forget the budget debit (sign_player doesn't debit) or the region sync, producing free signings or ladder-region desync. Needs a single wrapper to centralize it.

Benching via swap_roster_slots across many teams each mid-season tick risks thrash/log spam without the hysteresis + sample gates tuned; and applying swaps against a live (non-snapshot) roster can churn indices mid-loop — must batch.

Folding 5 strategy sites + 2 dead functions into one table is a broad (if mechanical) refactor touching Team.cpp and GameManager.cpp; behavior-preserving per step but a large diff to review, and any subtle weight change shifts league-wide roster outcomes that are hard to eyeball.

architectures.angle: STAGED-PIPELINE (FM scouting depth): three explicit layers per AI team — a STRATEGY layer (Contender/Rebuild/Bridge/TalentFarm + coach archetype) that sets risk appetite and a target window; a continuous SCOUTING layer that maintains a per-team shortlist of ranked targets refreshed monthly from FA/tier-2/solo-Q against a true role-need vector; and a TRANSACTIONS layer that executes signings/benchings/poaches against a per-role budget envelope and a confidence threshold. Clean separation of scout (rank what fits a need) vs decide (which moves, how many, what risk) vs act (route through sign_player/resign_player/swap_roster_slots). The strategy layer chooses HOW MANY moves and the confidence bar; the scout layer answers WHO fits; the transactions layer commits and respects every invariant.
architecture: LAYER A - STRATEGY (the 'decide risk appetite' brain). One pure function strategy_profile(Strategy, const Coach*) -> StrategyProfile collapses the 5 duplicated strategy switch-sites (CandidateRanker::strat_score Team.cpp:831, fill_one risk_bias 980-995, snake score_candidate GameManager.cpp:3014, decide_contract_years 1573, team_eval overlay GM:1863) into ONE table of weights {ovr_w, pot_w, youth_w, value_w, timeline_w, max_vet_age, min_pot_for_slot, risk_sign, max_signings, max_benchings, upgrade_delta, confidence_bar, contract_len_delta}. Coach archetype nudges the profile (DevelopmentCoach raises youth_w/pot_w + lowers max_vet_age; Pragmatist/AggressiveRiskTaker raise ovr_w + risk; VeteranMentor raises vet_retention). Strategy already classified + inertia-smoothed (classify_team_strategy Team.cpp:204, commit_strategy_with_inertia 2116) — feed coach into the SUGGESTED strategy so inertia still governs flips.

LAYER B - SCOUTING (the 'who fits a need' engine). New RosterPlan struct holds a true role-need vector computed BY DIFFERENCE (canonical D/C/I/S + demand-driven flex minus actual roster primary_role counts), NOT the strength-doubled pick_best_comp_for_roster label. New build_shortlist(const std::vector<PlayerPtr>& sources, const RosterPlan&, const StrategyProfile&) ranks every eligible candidate per OPEN need via ONE unified evaluate_signing(team, player, slot, incumbent) scorer = need_magnitude * (role_fit(slot) weighted) * (ovr_w*ovr + pot_w*pot + youth_w*youth + value_w*value_for_money + timeline_w*timeline_fit) + risk_sign*consistency + chemistry. Sources are merged: FA slice of solo_q ladder + top-N solo-Q FAs ranked by solo_mmr/peak_mmr + tier-2 risers (avg_match_rating/standing bar) flagged as POACH. Shortlist = top targets per need, stored on the team and refreshed monthly.

LAYER C - TRANSACTIONS (the 'commit moves' executor). New apply_transactions(RosterPlan&, shortlist, StrategyProfile&) walks needs largest-gap-first under a per-role BUDGET ENVELOPE (reserve cap across the 5 needs before any debit), and for each need: if a shortlisted target clears confidence_bar AND is an upgrade over the incumbent at that slot (evaluate delta >= upgrade_delta) AND affordable within that role's envelope AND accepts, commit via the right never-free mutator — FA -> sign_player; tier-2 riser -> release_from_source + sign_player + sync_player_ranked_region (B8 template); re-sign of own expiring -> resign_player. Move counts capped by profile.max_signings (WinNow buys more, Rebuild fewer/younger). All signings still flow through sign_player so IGL/Flex enforce + roster cap 7 hold.

BENCHING as a transactions sub-step: new optimize_starting_five(current_year) is the ONLY new mutator and it routes EXCLUSIVELY through existing swap_roster_slots (Team.cpp:732) so IGL/Flex re-election + still-paid-bench semantics come free. Snapshot-then-apply (collect desired swaps vs a snapshot, then apply) to avoid index/flag churn. For each bench reserve roster[5..] vs the weakest same-role starter, swap when reserve_value - starter_value >= profile.bench_margin (hysteresis ~+3-4, mirrors the +4 upgrade threshold). Guards: same primary_role only, protect young prospects (age<=21 && pot>=75), require season_matches>=6 before trusting form. Promote starter_value (Team.cpp:1711) to a shared free function so offseason pass, mid-season, and this pass share one 'how good right now' scorer.

ORCHESTRATION stays exactly as-is in shape — keep GameManager::run_end_of_year (1464) and run_lower_tier_offseason (4815) calling a single Team entrypoint (free_agents, year+1, log). ai_full_offseason_pass becomes a thin DRIVER: (Stage1) cut expired/poor [keep], (Stage2) profile = strategy_profile(strategy, head_coach); plan = compute_roster_plan(); (Stage3) shortlist = build_shortlist(merged_sources, plan, profile); (Stage4) apply_transactions(plan, shortlist, profile); (Stage5) optimize_starting_five(); (Stage6) report. auto_fill_roster is rewritten to consume the SAME plan (fill to the need vector, not the literal D/C/I/S template) and reused by apply_transactions for vacancy fills, so construction and upgrade share one need model.

MID-SEASON reuses the SAME three layers on the monthly tick (GameManager.cpp:1045-1052 / run_mid_season_replacements 1091). Instead of cut-and-replace-only, run: refresh plan -> refresh shortlist (now including tier-2 risers + solo-Q FAs, not just FA same-role) -> optimize_starting_five FIRST (prefer internal bench swap over external churn) -> then apply at most 1 confidence-gated transaction. Gate the user team behind user_auto_manage (add the missing skip at GM:1101). Same evaluate_signing + StrategyProfile, so mid-season and offseason judgement are coherent.

DEAD-CODE resolution: REVIVE the need-diff skeleton of scout_top_fas (Team.cpp:765, target_comp.need-have at 770) by folding it into compute_roster_plan/build_shortlist, then DELETE the standalone scout_top_fas + ai_manage_roster (1511) and their Team.h decls (363,395) so there is ONE authoritative path. CandidateRanker is kept as reusable scoring infra but is now fed a real role-need + budget-envelope + coach context via evaluate_signing rather than being called raw.
data_model: struct StrategyProfile { double ovr_w, pot_w, youth_w, value_w, timeline_w, risk_sign; int max_vet_age, min_pot_for_slot, max_signings, max_benchings; double upgrade_delta, bench_margin, confidence_bar; int contract_len_delta; }; — pure value type, no behavior. Free function StrategyProfile strategy_profile(Team::Strategy, const Coach* coach) in Team.cpp. KEEP-COMPILING: additive new struct + new function; the 5 existing strategy switch-sites are refactored to read fields off this struct but their call signatures are unchanged, so every caller still compiles. Default-construct gives Bridge-like neutral weights so any un-migrated site is safe.

struct RoleNeed { Role role; int gap; bool core_empty; double incumbent_value; bool is_poach_only; }; and struct RosterPlan { std::array<int,Role::Count> required{}; std::array<int,Role::Count> have{}; std::vector<RoleNeed> needs; std::array<long long,Role::Count> budget_envelope{}; }; — needs sorted largest-gap-first, core_empty (have==0) prioritized over doubled-surplus. KEEP-COMPILING: brand-new structs in a new header section or top of Team.cpp; nothing existing references them yet, so introduction is non-breaking. budget_envelope reserves cap per role before any debit (fixes the greedy-debit starvation at Team.cpp:1053).

struct ShortlistEntry { PlayerPtr player; Role fills_role; double score; double eval_delta_over_incumbent; SourceKind source; }; enum class SourceKind { FreeAgent, SoloQRiser, Tier2Poach }; and a per-team member std::vector<ShortlistEntry> shortlist_; on Team (Team.h, near target_comp). KEEP-COMPILING: new member default-inits empty; no existing serialization/ctor touches it, existing call sites unaffected. Refreshed by build_shortlist, consumed by apply_transactions + mid-season; survives between monthly refreshes so it is a true continuous scouting layer.

double evaluate_signing(const Team& t, const Player& p, Role slot, const Player* incumbent, const StrategyProfile& prof) — the single unified scorer; pure/O(1) so it is safe in the snake-draft inner loop. Folds: magnitude-scaled need, role_fit_score(slot) (wires the currently-dead off-role compatibility blend Player.cpp:161 into acquisition), value_for_money via market_value_estimate (Team.cpp:2427), timeline_fit_score (2235), and (candidate - incumbent) delta so EVERY path scores relative to the depth chart. KEEP-COMPILING: new free function; the four legacy scorers (score_candidate, fill_one composite, scout_top_fas, Stage-2 starter_value) are migrated to call it one at a time — until migrated they keep their own formula and still compile.

Free function double starter_value_now(const Player&) hoisted out of the Team.cpp:1711 lambda (ovr + age<=24:(pot-ovr)*0.30 + form*8 - age>=30:5) into file scope so optimize_starting_five, ai_full_offseason_pass Stage-2, and run_mid_season_replacements share ONE 'how good right now' scorer. KEEP-COMPILING: identical formula, just lifted; the existing lambda call site is replaced by the free-function call, byte-equivalent behavior so nothing downstream shifts.

New methods on Team (decls in Team.h, defs in Team.cpp): RosterPlan compute_roster_plan() const; void build_shortlist(const std::vector<PlayerPtr>& sources, const RosterPlan&, const StrategyProfile&); void apply_transactions(RosterPlan&, const StrategyProfile&, std::vector<PlayerPtr>& sources, int year, std::vector<std::string>& log); void optimize_starting_five(int current_year, const StrategyProfile&); — plus a GameManager helper std::vector<PlayerPtr> gather_scout_sources(region) that merges FA ladder + top-N solo-Q + tier-2 risers (flagged). KEEP-COMPILING: all additive; ai_full_offseason_pass/auto_fill_roster signatures are UNCHANGED so run_end_of_year:2284 and run_lower_tier_offseason:4875 call sites compile untouched.
decision_flow: STEP 1 SET STRATEGY: at year-end, classify_team_strategy(t) -> suggested, nudged by head_coach archetype, then commit_strategy_with_inertia smooths the flip (existing path GM:2302). prof = strategy_profile(t.strategy, t.head_coach). This fixes the per-window/strategy coherence and gives Contender vs Rebuild genuinely different max_signings, confidence_bar, max_vet_age, upgrade_delta.

STEP 2 PLAN NEEDS: plan = compute_roster_plan(). Build have[] from roster primary_role; required[] = canonical 1-per-core-role + ONE demand-driven flex slot (the thinnest core role / the role the roster's role_fit can least cover) — NOT the strength-doubled tag. Emit needs where have<required, sorted core_empty-first then largest-gap-first. This is the #1 'wrong roles' fix: a 1-duelist/0-sentinel roster now yields a Sentinel need, never a 2nd-Duelist need.

STEP 3 RESERVE BUDGET: split affordable budget into budget_envelope[role] across the open needs (weighted by gap severity + profile), BEFORE any signing, so an early Duelist can't starve the Sentinel slot into the solo_mmr fallback. Envelopes are spent down as transactions commit.

STEP 4 SCOUT / SHORTLIST: build_shortlist over merged sources (FA ladder slice + top-N solo-Q-by-MMR FAs + tier-2 risers flagged Tier2Poach). For each open need, score every eligible candidate with evaluate_signing(t, cand, need.role, incumbent_at_slot, prof). Off-role-but-high-role_fit candidates are now scoreable (penalized, not forbidden) — fixes the soft-filter bug where an empty Sentinel slot silently signed a 2nd Duelist. Take top-K per need into shortlist_.

STEP 5 ACT / TRANSACT: walk needs in priority order under their envelope. For each, take the best shortlist entry; commit ONLY if score >= prof.confidence_bar AND eval_delta_over_incumbent >= prof.upgrade_delta AND fits envelope AND candidate accepts (desire_accepts) AND import-cap/roster-cap OK. Route by source: FreeAgent/SoloQRiser -> budget-=value; sign_player(...,decide_contract_years). Tier2Poach -> source_team->release_player(p); sign_player; sync_player_ranked_region if cross-region (B8 never-free template). Own expiring upgrade -> resign_player in-place. Stop when prof.max_signings reached.

STEP 6 OPTIMIZE STARTERS (BENCH BY MERIT): optimize_starting_five(year, prof) snapshots roster, computes starter_value_now for each starter + each same-role reserve, and for any reserve beating the weakest same-role starter by >= prof.bench_margin records a swap (skip protected young prospects, require season_matches>=6). Apply all collected swaps via swap_roster_slots only — IGL/Flex stay legal automatically. Runs in offseason AND mid-season.

STEP 7 MID-SEASON LOOP (monthly tick): for each AI team (skip user unless user_auto_manage), re-run STEP 2 (refresh plan from current roster, not stale tag) -> STEP 6 FIRST (prefer internal bench swap over external churn) -> then STEP 4/5 capped at 1 confidence-gated move (bench a slumping starter for a tier-2 riser/solo-Q FA only when it fills a need AND upgrades). This is the continuous scouting layer in action: shortlist refreshed monthly, transactions gated by the same confidence bar.

STEP 8 REPORT + INVARIANTS: every mutating path already ends in enforce_one_igl/enforce_one_flex via sign_player/release_player/swap_roster_slots, so the final starting 5 has exactly 1 IGL and >=1 Flex by construction. Emit news/log per move (BENCH vs SIGN vs POACH-FROM-TIER-2) reusing push_news 'Roster Move'. Delete dead scout_top_fas/ai_manage_roster once their need-diff logic is harvested into compute_roster_plan.
pros: Maximum FM-depth and the cleanest separation of concerns: scout (build_shortlist) vs decide (StrategyProfile + RosterPlan priority) vs act (apply_transactions/optimize_starting_five) are three independently testable units, each with a single responsibility — easiest to reason about and extend of any angle.

Directly kills the two root 'wrong role' bugs: the need vector is a true vacancy DIFF (not strength-doubled pick_best_comp_for_roster), and the soft role-filter is replaced by magnitude-scaled role_fit scoring, so an empty Sentinel can never be filled by a 2nd Duelist and the demand-driven flex replaces the flat 1-1-1-1-1 template.

Genuine short- and long-term strategy: StrategyProfile makes WinNow/Contender buy more proven win-now talent at a lower confidence bar while Rebuild/Dev/TalentFarm cap vet age, raise youth/potential weight, and bench/keep prospects — strategy now GATES move count + confidence, not just nudges a blended score.

Coach archetypes finally shape acquisitions, not just cut cadence — strategy_profile folds coach youth/proven/vet-retention/risk into the same weights that drive evaluate_signing, decide_contract_years, and import appetite, so a DevelopmentCoach visibly builds youth and a Pragmatist buys proven OVR.

Merit-based benching (offseason + mid-season) via the existing swap_roster_slots with hysteresis + prospect/sample guards — a rostered reserve who is clearly better now starts, and the engine prefers an internal bench over releasing depth.

Full scouting pipeline: tier-2 risers (by avg_match_rating/standing) and top solo-Q FAs (by solo_mmr/peak_mmr) become first-class targets, signed ONLY when they fill a real need and clear the upgrade/confidence bar, via the never-free B8 release-then-sign template — exactly the requested behavior.

Budget envelope across the 5 needs eliminates the greedy-debit starvation that dropped late roles into the random solo_mmr fallback; unifying four divergent scorers into evaluate_signing makes desirability coherent across draft, fill, scout, upgrade, and B8.

Strictly invariant-safe and call-site-stable: every mutation still flows through sign_player/release_player/resign_player/swap_roster_slots (IGL/Flex + roster cap 7 + never-free), and ai_full_offseason_pass/auto_fill_roster signatures are unchanged so run_end_of_year and run_lower_tier_offseason compile untouched.
cons: Largest surface area of the candidate angles: it touches strategy classification, two rewritten Team methods, a new shortlist member + struct family, a new GameManager scout-source merger, mid-season, AND the dead-code deletion — the most code to get right and to keep the build green through.

A persistent shortlist_ member adds per-team state that must be refreshed/invalidated correctly (monthly + offseason); a stale shortlist holding a now-signed or retired PlayerPtr could mislead transactions if refresh ordering is wrong — needs careful 'rebuild before act' discipline.

The budget envelope + confidence-bar + per-role priority introduces several new tunables (envelope split weights, confidence_bar, upgrade_delta, bench_margin, max_signings per strategy) that need sim-testing to avoid either over-trading (thrash) or frozen rosters; getting the numbers FM-believable is iterative.

Tier-2 poaching can drain lower tiers if uncapped; the per-team poach cap mitigates it but the cross-tier release-then-sign + sync_player_ranked_region path is new machinery with no existing helper, so it is the riskiest never-free sequence to implement correctly.

Migrating five legacy scorers onto one evaluate_signing must be done incrementally to keep compiling, meaning a transitional period where some paths use the new scorer and some the old — behavior is coherent only after the full migration, so partial landings can look inconsistent.

Mid-season now does more per monthly tick (plan refresh + shortlist rebuild + optimize_starting_five + a transaction) across every AI team — heavier per-tick cost than today's single worst-starter scan; evaluate_signing must stay O(1) and source-gathering bounded to avoid sim slowdown.

Order per year: (a) year-end news/awards/finance/coach-dev loop (1467-1556); (b) Deep re-sign + market-competition pass (B7/B8) starting ~1757 — each team's expiring players compute an ask via propose_resign_offer and the team counter-offers, accepts_resign_offer gates it (1942-2012); (c) FA POACH pass — up to 3 rival teams bid on players who walked, willingness*amount ranks the winner (2099-2180); (d) roster CLEANUP pass releases expired (exp_year<=year), retired, or agitating (discontent>=0.85, AI only) players (2188-2226); (e) resolve_promotion_relegation (2241); (f) MAIN REBUILD LOOP at 2243-2312: for every tier-1 team, save_history, reset W/L, ensure head_coach, then if (t != user_team || user_auto_manage) call t->ai_full_offseason_pass(fa_pool, year+1, log) (2284); afterwards refresh_target_comp (2286), prestige/sponsorship recompute, classify_team_strategy + commit_strategy_with_inertia (2302-2303); (g) generate_schedule; (h) run_lower_tier_offseason (2320).

ORCHESTRATION (tiers 2+): GameManager::run_lower_tier_offseason (GameManager.cpp:4815). Per lower-tier team: re-sign in place any expiring player with rating>=0.90 OR potential>=70 OR ovr>=cap-8 (4844-4855), drop the rest + retired, then t->ai_full_offseason_pass(sub, year+1, log) (4875), then classify_team_strategy + enforce_one_igl + enforce_one_flex (4876-4878). FA sub-pool is region-local and ovr-capped by tier (4827-4834).

ai_full_offseason_pass (Team.cpp:1631) — the actual per-team GM brain, 5 stages: Stage 0 log header (1634-1652). Stage 1 CUT: drop players where (career_matches>10 && avg_match_rating<threshold && potential<65) OR years_left<=0; threshold = 0.85 baseline, 0.80 if stability_culture>=40, 0.90 if <=-40; chemistry sum>=2.0 protects a poor (non-expired) player (1654-1700). Stage 2 UPGRADE: for each starter slot 0..4, if not protected (rating>=1.10 skip; age<=22 && pot>=75 skip), scan FAs of THE SAME primary_role for one with starter_value delta>=+4, cost<=40% budget, who accepts; commit ONE cut-and-replace max (kMaxUpgradesPerPass=1, 1724) then resets slot=0 (1702-1798). Stage 3 BACKSTOP: if roster<5 call auto_fill_roster (1800-1809). Stage 4 BENCH: if exactly 5 starters && budget>=400K, sign one depth piece scored potential*0.6+ovr*0.4-ask*0.5, skipping any role already stacked 2+ (1811-1865). Stage 5 report (1867-1877).

auto_fill_roster (Team.cpp:896) — the squad CONSTRUCTION engine. Fills to 5 in fixed role order via fill_one closure: slot1 Duelist, slot2 Controller, slot3 Initiator, slot4 Sentinel, each guarded by has_role() so it only fills a core role the starting-5 lacks (1071-1075). Slot 5 cascade: try a is_flex FA of any role -> else 2nd Initiator -> else 2nd Controller -> else best remaining FA (1077-1086). Pathological while-loop fills any remaining vacancy with best FA (1093-1095). Ends with enforce_one_igl, enforce_one_flex, refresh_target_comp (1102-1104).

fill_one candidate selection (Team.cpp:905-1056): collect Free-Agent pool; import gate (team_import_appetite * 0.45 coin flip, max_imports cap, import FAs must be ovr>=60) else filter to regional; quality filter ovr>=40; role filter to wanted_role; optional is_flex overlay filter; affordability filter (get_transfer_value<=budget); then rank affordable by CandidateRanker.total (50% comp_fit + 35% strategy + 15% personality, Team.cpp:868-872) PLUS 0.40*timeline_fit + risk_bias (consistency_mod*120 for contenders, -cm*80 for rebuilders w/ pot>=80) + 8.0*chemistry reunite (972-1011); sort desc; desire-aware acceptance scan picks first candidate whose demand<=40% budget AND desire_accepts, else top-ranked (1032-1043); fallback = highest solo_mmr if nothing affordable (1044-1050). Signs via sign_player + decide_contract_years (1053-1054).

Number of moves per team per offseason: cuts = unbounded (everyone expired/poor), upgrades = max 1 (kMaxUpgradesPerPass), fills = however many slots are vacant up to 5, bench = max 1. Keep/cut decision is the Stage-1 rule above; sign decision is the CandidateRanker composite + desire acceptance.

gaps: ROLE-FILL IS POSITION-BLIND TO PLAYER QUALITY VS COMP: auto_fill_roster (Team.cpp:1071-1075) always fills the literal D/C/I/S template regardless of target_comp/identity. target_comp is computed AFTER filling (refresh_target_comp at 1104) FROM whoever got signed, and pick_best_comp_for_roster (1107) just reads roster attribute averages — so the comp never DRIVES who is signed; it is a trailing label. The CompIdentity (DoubleDuelistTeam, UtilityHeavy, etc.) is only consulted in-match (decide_desired_tag, 1166) and never in roster construction. Result: a 'Double Duelist' identity team still builds a flat 1-1-1-1-1.

WRONG ROLE WHEN A ROLE FA IS MISSING: in fill_one (Team.cpp:939-943) the role filter is SOFT — if no FA of wanted_role exists in the (already region/quality-narrowed) pool, role_fas is empty and the code KEEPS the unfiltered `valid` set, then signs the best-ranked player of ANY role. So 'fill the Sentinel slot' silently signs a 2nd Duelist. has_role (1059) then still reports the core role as unfilled, but slots are one-shot (no retry for that specific role) so the team ends with duplicate roles and a hole. This is the primary 'fills wrong roles' bug.

STARTER-SLOT INDEXING IS FRAGILE: ai_full_offseason_pass Stage 2 treats roster[0..4] as positional 'slots' and same-role upgrades (1726-1734), but sign_player only push_back's (Team.cpp:422) and release_player erase+shifts (714-730). After a cut-and-replace it resets slot=0 and re-scans (1789), and enforce_one_igl/flex can reorder via stat election — so 'slot' has no stable meaning; the upgrade loop can thrash or re-evaluate the same player.

ONLY 1 UPGRADE + 1 BENCH PER YEAR, PURELY REACTIVE: kMaxUpgradesPerPass=1 (1724) and bench cap 1 (1822) mean a bad roster needs MANY years to fix itself and never makes a coordinated multi-move plan. Strategy (WinNow/Rebuild) only tweaks the ranker weights (strat_score 831-852) and contract length; it never changes HOW MANY moves or WHICH roles — no real squad-construction strategy, matching the user's 'no strategy' complaint.

NO BUDGET / CAP PLANNING ACROSS MOVES: fills debit budget greedily in fixed order (1053), so an early Duelist can eat the budget and leave the Sentinel slot filled by the solo_mmr fallback (1044-1050) — a random worst-case pick. There is no reservation of cap for the 5 needed roles.

DEAD STRATEGIC SCOUT LOGIC: scout_top_fas (765) actually DOES comp-need-driven, scout-pref-weighted signing (the kind of strategy the user wants) but is never called; the live path threw it away in favor of the template fill. ai_manage_roster (1511) is also dead. Two parallel unused implementations create confusion about which is authoritative.

IMPORT GATE IS A RANDOM COIN FLIP: fill_one (915-917) gates imports on rng().chance(appetite*0.45); a team that needs the best available Sentinel may randomly refuse to even look at imports, signing a weaker regional or falling to the any-role fallback.

COMP_FIT ONLY HAS 4 DOUBLE-X SHAPES: init_comps (Agent.cpp:110-113) and comp_fit_score (31) model only DoubleDuelist/Initiator/Controller/Sentinel. There is no notion of agent-specific or map-pool needs, so 'fit' is a coarse attribute average — shallow vs FM-depth role/position/role-familiarity modeling.
overhaul_notes: Single rewrite surface: replace Team::auto_fill_roster (Team.cpp:896) and Team::ai_full_offseason_pass (Team.cpp:1631). Make comp/identity DRIVE construction: compute the desired role-need vector (from comp_identity + target_comp + map pool) FIRST, then fill to that vector — do NOT call refresh_target_comp last as a trailing label. The dead scout_top_fas (765) already has the need-driven skeleton (target_comp.need - have at 770) to harvest.

Fix the soft-role-filter bug: in fill_one (Team.cpp:939-943) when no FA of wanted_role exists, either skip the slot (leave vacancy for the pathological loop / next year) or widen with an explicit penalty — never silently sign an off-role player while has_role still reports the role unfilled. This is the #1 'wrong roles' fix.

Make moves/strategy a plan, not a per-slot greedy loop: lift kMaxUpgradesPerPass (1724) and bench cap (1822) into a strategy-driven budget (WinNow buys more, Rebuild trades for youth/picks). Stop using roster[0..4] as positional slots (unstable due to push_back/erase/IGL election) — key upgrades by primary_role, not index.
Budget should be reserved across the 5 role needs before any signing (currently greedy debit at 1053 can starve later roles into the solo_mmr fallback at 1044-1050). Add a per-role budget envelope.

Constraint to preserve (never-free invariant + IGL/Flex): all roster mutation MUST go through sign_player / release_player (Team.cpp:407/714) which run enforce_one_igl (538) + enforce_one_flex (660). Any new construction code must end with exactly 1 IGL and >=1 Flex among the starting 5. roster cap is 7 (sign_player 417), starters are first 5.

Orchestration is the right shape — keep run_end_of_year (1464) and run_lower_tier_offseason (4815) calling a single Team:: entrypoint with (free_agents, year+1, log). The user team is excluded unless user_auto_manage (2277). The re-sign (1757+) and poach (2099+) passes already run BEFORE the rebuild, so the rebuild entrypoint can assume expiring/poached players are already resolved and roster holds only kept players.

Decide whether to delete or revive the two dead functions (scout_top_fas 765, ai_manage_roster 1511) and their Team.h declarations (363, 395) so there is one authoritative path. CandidateRanker (826-873) is reusable scoring infra — keep it but feed it a real role-need + budget-envelope context.

Watch contract-year semantics: callers pass year+1 because signings are for the upcoming season (sign_player stamps exp_year = sign_year+years-1, 454); decide_contract_years (1554) is the shared length brain and should remain the single source for offered length.

maps.component: Team role-need determination and role-filling (Team.cpp::scout_top_fas / auto_fill_roster / pick_best_comp_for_roster, Agent.cpp comp plumbing, GameManager.cpp B8 market bidding)
current_behavior: NEED is stored as Team::target_comp, a CompPlan = std::array<int,Role::Count> need{} (include/Agent.h:41-43). It is set three ways: (1) ctor random_comp() (Team.cpp:81); (2) refresh_target_comp() -> comp_by_tag(pick_best_comp_for_roster()) called after auto_fill_roster (Team.cpp:1104,1152) and in GameManager:2286; (3) never otherwise mutated.

There are ONLY 4 valid comps, all 1/1/1/1/1 with exactly ONE role bucket doubled to 2 (Agent.cpp init_comps 110-113: add(1,2,1,1)=DoubleInit, add(2,1,1,1)=DoubleDuelist, add(1,1,2,1)=DoubleController, add(1,1,1,2)=DoubleSentinel). So target_comp.need[] is always {1,1,1,1} with one entry =2.

pick_best_comp_for_roster (Team.cpp:1107-1150) decides WHICH role to double purely from roster-AVERAGE attributes: duelist_signal=(Aggr+Entry+Aim)/n, initiator=(Util+Comm+GS)/n, controller=(Intel+Dec+Lead)/n, sentinel=(Pos+Clutch+Adapt)/n, plus a flat personality bump (+6/+6/+4/+3 at 1137-1142). argmax of the 4 signals -> CompTag. It does NOT look at which primary_roles are actually missing from the roster.

INITIAL fill (auto_fill_roster, Team.cpp:896-1105) is correct: slots 1-4 each guarded by has_role(Role) on primary_role (1072-1075), so it signs exactly one Duelist/Controller/Initiator/Sentinel; slot 5 is a flex cascade (1078-1086). has_role (1059-1065) checks roster[i]->primary_role across the starting <=5.

IN-SEASON fill via scout_top_fas (Team.cpp:765-808): builds missing[] by need = target_comp.need[i]-have[i] over roles in index order 0..3 (768-772, i.e. Duelist,Initiator,Controller,Sentinel), pushing the role 'need' times; then for each missing role front-to-back signs ONLY a FA whose p->primary_role==target_role exactly (779) ranked by scout_prefs (786-797). No off-role candidates are ever considered.

IN-SEASON star-poach market (GameManager.cpp B8, 2041-2118): a rival bids on a walked star only if other->target_comp.need[role_idx] > have for that star's primary_role (2068-2074). So a team whose target_comp doubles Duelist will out-bid for a SECOND duelist star even with an empty Sentinel slot.

The new compatibility-driven role_fit_score (Player.cpp:161-199, weighted attribute blend, 1.00 if r==primary_role) and role_fit_penalty_mult are used ONLY in build_round_selection (per-map in-GAME agent assignment, Team.cpp:1317,1326,1494) and in negotiation role-switch weighting (Player.cpp:3170). It is NOT used anywhere in roster construction / FA scoring, so off-role signings are impossible in the fill paths.

gaps: WRONG ROLE root cause: need is derived from pick_best_comp_for_roster (Team.cpp:1107-1150), which doubles whichever role the roster is ALREADY strongest in (highest attribute average). A duelist-heavy roster gets target_comp=DoubleDuelist -> need[Duelist]=2. If it has 1 duelist and 0 sentinels, scout_top_fas (770) computes need[Duelist]=2-1=1 and need[Sentinel]=1-0=1; it iterates roles in index order (Duelist idx0 before Sentinel idx3, 768-772) and signs the Duelist FIRST. Net effect: the team reinforces its strength (2nd Duelist) instead of fixing its hole (Sentinel). This is exactly the user-reported 'signs a 2nd Duelist when it needs a Sentinel.'

The system literally cannot express 'I need a Sentinel because I have none' — need is a fixed 1/1/1/1 template + one doubled role chosen by strength, NOT a diff between the canonical D/C/I/S/flex shape and the actual roster's missing primary_roles. A roster of {Duelist,Duelist,Duelist,Initiator,Controller} has need[Sentinel]=1, have=0, but ALSO need[Duelist]=2, have=3 (negative) — the only positive gaps are Sentinel(1) and maybe Controller; yet pick_best_comp_for_roster would still tag DoubleDuelist, and refresh runs only after fills, so mid-season the stale tag drives more duelist appetite.

scout_top_fas and B8 are strict on-role only (Team.cpp:779, GameManager.cpp:2068-2074): an excellent flex/off-role player who role_fit_score=0.82 for Sentinel will never be signed to fill a Sentinel hole because primary_role!=Sentinel. The compatibility scoring you just built is dead code for acquisition — it only affects in-match agent assignment.

Need ordering is positional, not severity-weighted: missing[] is filled in Role enum order (Duelist,Initiator,Controller,Sentinel), and scout_top_fas pops missing.front() (775). There is no 'largest gap first' or 'unfilled core role (have==0) before doubling' priority, so a doubled-role surplus is pursued before a zero-filled core role.

personality flat bumps (Team.cpp:1137-1142: Aggressive +6 duelist) can flip the comp tag toward a role the roster does not need, compounding the strength-bias with a personality-bias, again ignoring actual vacancies.

auto_fill_roster (the GOOD path) and scout_top_fas (the BAD path) use two different definitions of need: auto_fill uses has_role/primary_role presence (correct one-per-core-role), scout_top_fas uses target_comp.need diff (strength-doubled). They disagree, so initial rosters look right but in-season churn drifts toward duplicated roles.
overhaul_notes: Replace the 'doubled-role from strength' need model with a true vacancy diff: define the canonical requirement as the 5-slot D/C/I/S + flex shape, compute have[role] from roster primary_role, and emit gaps where have < required. Make the FLEX/2nd slot demand-driven (which core role is thinnest / which role the roster's role_fit can least cover) rather than 'whatever we're already best at'. pick_best_comp_for_roster (Team.cpp:1107) is the right single chokepoint to rewrite, since both target_comp consumers read its output.

Severity-order the missing[] queue in scout_top_fas (Team.cpp:768-775): prioritize roles with have==0 (empty core role) over roles whose need is just a doubled surplus. Sort gaps by (required-have) desc, breaking ties by how poorly the existing roster can flex-cover that role via role_fit_score.

Wire the new compatibility-driven role_fit_score (Player.cpp:161) into acquisition. In scout_top_fas (779) and fill_one's role filter (Team.cpp:939-943), stop hard-requiring primary_role==target; instead score candidates by max(on_role_bonus, role_fit_score(target_role)*weight) so a strong off-role/flex can fill a hole. Reuse role_fit_penalty_mult so off-role signings are dinged, not forbidden.

Apply the same gap-aware gate to the B8 market (GameManager.cpp:2068-2074): a rival should only out-bid for a role it genuinely lacks (have < required AND that role is a real vacancy, not a strength-double), and should weight by role_fit-coverable need so it does not chase a redundant star.

Constraint: target_comp.need[] must stay summing to 5 with the 1-doubled shape ONLY if you keep comp_tag_of / comp_by_tag (Agent.cpp:205-214) and the per-map Flex-role logic intact — those decode the doubled bucket as the Flex's agent role. If you change need[] semantics for acquisition, decouple 'acquisition need' from 'per-map comp plan' (introduce a separate roster_gap_vector) so build_round_selection's plan.need (Team.cpp:1345-1352) keeps its current 5-role demand meaning.

refresh_target_comp is only called AFTER fills (Team.cpp:1104) and once in GameManager:2286, so mid-season signing decisions run against a stale/strength-biased tag. Recompute the gap vector at the START of each acquisition pass (before scout_top_fas/B8) so need reflects the current roster, not last refresh.

maps.component: Team Strategy system (Team::Strategy / TeamWindow / classify_team_strategy / decide_contract_years) and its (non-)integration into roster decisions in cpp/src/Team.cpp, cpp/include/Team.h, cpp/src/GameManager.cpp
current_behavior: FULL ENUM (7 values, single byte): Team.h:126-134 `enum class Strategy : std::uint8_t { Contender, Rebuilding, Bridge, BudgetRoster, DevelopmentFocus, WinNow, TalentFarm }`. Default `strategy = Strategy::Bridge` (Team.h:135). Names at Team.cpp:178-189, blurbs at Team.cpp:191-202.
SEPARATE enum TeamWindow (Team.h:66-68): `{ Opening, Open, Closing, Closed }`, member `window` (Team.h:155). This is the OTHER lifecycle axis and is more wired-in than Strategy in several places.

CLASSIFICATION (Team.cpp:204-230 `classify_team_strategy`): pure decision tree on roster averages (avg_age, avg_pot), `t.ovr()`, `budget`, `prestige`. First-match cascade: WinNow if bud>=1.5M & ovr>=75 & prestige>=70; Contender if bud>=900K & ovr>=72; Rebuilding if avg_age<=21 & avg_pot>=78; DevelopmentFocus if avg_age<=22 & avg_pot>=70; BudgetRoster if bud<400K; TalentFarm if avg_pot>=80 & bud<600K; else Bridge. Note TalentFarm is nearly unreachable (BudgetRoster at bud<400K shadows most of its budget range).

ASSIGNMENT: at world init GameManager.cpp:157,180 and 4576,4605 set `strategy = classify_team_strategy(*t)` and seed `previous_strategy = strategy`. At year-end GameManager.cpp:2302-2303 computes `suggested = classify_team_strategy` then `strategy = commit_strategy_with_inertia(*t, suggested)`.

STRATEGY INERTIA (Team.cpp:2116-2161 `commit_strategy_with_inertia`): 7x7 probabilistic transition matrix M[from][to]; if suggested==previous, no-op; else roll rng().chance(M[from][to]) to accept new vs keep previous. Resists philosophy flips.
WINDOW classification (Team.cpp:2177-2225 `compute_team_window`): cascade on avg_age/avg_pot/avg_ovr/avg_yrs of starters. Recomputed year-end at GameManager.cpp:1753 BEFORE strategy reclassify.

WHERE STRATEGY ACTUALLY DRIVES DECISIONS (5 sites, all shallow score-nudges): (1) Recruitment ranker CandidateRanker::strat_score Team.cpp:831-854 — Contender/WinNow score ovr*1.4+rating; Rebuilding/DevFocus score potential*1.5+youth; weighted 0.35 of total (Team.cpp:868-872). (2) auto_fill_roster risk_bias Team.cpp:980-995 — Contenders +consistency, Rebuilders -consistency if pot>=80. (3) Snake-draft score_candidate GameManager.cpp:3014-3032 + risk_bias 3080-3088 — same shape as ranker. (4) decide_contract_years Stage 3 Team.cpp:1573-1582 — WinNow -0.5y, Rebuilding +0.7y, DevFocus +0.9y, etc. (5) Re-sign team_eval strategy overlay GameManager.cpp:1863-1870 — Contender/WinNow +20 if ovr>=80; Rebuild/TalentFarm -25 if age>=28. Plus financial_discipline baseline Team.cpp:2015-2022 and import appetite Team.cpp:262-285.

decide_contract_years (Team.cpp:1554-1629): 9 stages blending player length-pref, window (1561-1571), strategy (1573-1582), age, potential-gap, org memory, budget headroom; clamps [1,4] then caps to player tolerance.

BUDGET / ECONOMY (GameManager.cpp:1515-1545): each year-start budget += sponsorship, pay_payroll, minus op_cost, then HARD soft-cap at $3M (1535-1537). No banking/saving toward a future star — surplus is shaved. No multi-year budget projection (decide_contract_years current_year param is explicitly `(void)`'d at 1555).
