gaps: THE BIG ONE — offseason upgrade pass is strategy-BLIND. ai_full_offseason_pass Stage 2 (Team.cpp:1702-1798) is the main yearly roster-mutation engine, and it makes cut-and-replace decisions purely on `starter_value` = OVR + small youth/age/form terms (1711-1717) with a flat `+4 OVR` delta threshold (1738). `strategy` is never read in this entire stage. So a Rebuilding team and a WinNow team upgrade identically: both chase the highest raw-OVR FA. This is exactly the 'no long-term nor short-term thinking' the user reports — every team behaves like a generic win-marginally-now team in the offseason.

Bench-depth Stage 4 (Team.cpp:1811-1865) is also strategy-blind: scores `potential*0.6+ovr*0.4-cost` for everyone. A Contender should buy a proven veteran bench insurance; a TalentFarm should hoard cheap high-pot prospects to flip. Currently identical.

Strategy only ever NUDGES a blended score (0.35 weight in CandidateRanker Team.cpp:870; 0.6 timeline weight; small risk_bias) and never GATES. There is no branch anywhere that says 'Rebuilder: do NOT sign a 30-year-old vet even if he is +6 OVR' or 'Contender: do NOT burn a roster slot on an 18-yo project'. The +20/-25 overlays (GameManager.cpp:1863-1870) only fire at extreme ovr>=80 / age>=28 edges, so 90% of candidates see zero strategy effect.

No buy-vs-develop fork. There is no concept of 'this strategy prefers internal development (keep youngster, spend coach budget) over external buy'. ai_full_offseason_pass always tries to BUY upgrades regardless of strategy; DevelopmentFocus/TalentFarm should bias toward keeping and growing young players, but the protect-young guard (Team.cpp:1732 age<=22 & pot>=75) is a flat rule, not strategy-driven.

No long-term resource planning. Budget surplus is actively destroyed by the $3M soft cap (GameManager.cpp:1535-1537); teams cannot save up for a marquee signing. decide_contract_years explicitly discards current_year (Team.cpp:1555) so there is zero forward budget projection — contract length never considers 'can I still afford this in year 3'.

Window vs Strategy are computed independently and can contradict (e.g. Strategy=Contender but Window=Closed) with no reconciliation; downstream code reads whichever happens to be wired at that site, producing incoherent behavior.

TalentFarm is essentially dead: classify_team_strategy (Team.cpp:227-228) reaches BudgetRoster (bud<400K) before TalentFarm (avg_pot>=80 & bud<600K), so most would-be TalentFarms are tagged BudgetRoster. Its sell-for-profit intent is never implemented anywhere — no code ever flips a developed prospect for cash.
overhaul_notes: PRIMARY HOOK: inject strategy into ai_full_offseason_pass (Team.cpp:1631-1878). Make `starter_value` (1711-1717) and the `+4 delta` threshold (1738) strategy-dependent: WinNow/Contender lower the threshold and weight current OVR + form heavily (buy win-now); Rebuilding/DevelopmentFocus raise the threshold for veterans and instead bias the protect-young guard (1731-1732) to NEVER cut a high-pot youngster, and prefer signing young upside over an older +4-OVR vet. Add an explicit buy-vs-develop branch BEFORE the FA scan: for dev-strategies, first try internal (keep + coach), only buy if a genuine youth upgrade exists.

Convert strategy from score-nudge to GATE at the candidate-filter level. In auto_fill::fill_one (Team.cpp:935-958) and snake-draft score_candidate (GameManager.cpp:2996+), add hard filters/large penalties: Rebuilding/DevFocus should HARD-drop age>=28 unless a star; Contender/WinNow should HARD-drop low-pot benchwarmers with no immediate OVR. The CandidateRanker (Team.cpp:826-873) already centralizes scoring for both auto_fill and offseason fill — extend it with a `bool acceptable(const Player&)` gate so every signing path inherits the same strategy guardrails.

Reconcile Strategy and Window. They are computed back-to-back at year-end (window at GameManager.cpp:1753, strategy at 2302). Either fold window into classify_team_strategy or add a coherence pass so Contender+Closed downgrades to Bridge/WinNow-shortcommit. timeline_fit_score (Team.cpp:2235) is the existing window-aware multi-year scorer — reuse it as the backbone and have strategy choose the WEIGHT on it (Rebuilder weights timeline_fit high, WinNow weights it ~0).

Add long-term thinking primitives: (a) give decide_contract_years (Team.cpp:1554) real use of current_year + a projected per-year budget so it won't hand out 4y deals a Rebuilder can't sustain; (b) relax/remove the $3M soft-cap destruction (GameManager.cpp:1535-1537) OR add a `saving_target` so Contenders can bank for a marquee FA; (c) add a per-team multi-year roster plan field (e.g. target window-year) that strategies steer toward.

Implement TalentFarm for real: fix the classify ordering (Team.cpp:227-228 — test TalentFarm before BudgetRoster) and add a SELL action in ai_full_offseason_pass: TalentFarm teams with a developed prospect (pot-ovr gap closed, ovr high) should release/sell for budget rather than keep. Today no sell path exists.

All five existing strategy sites already share the SAME shape (Contender/WinNow vs Rebuild/DevFocus/TalentFarm grouping): CandidateRanker.strat_score (Team.cpp:833-852), auto_fill risk_bias (980-994), snake score_candidate (3014-3032) + risk_bias (3080-3087), decide_contract_years (1574-1582), team_eval overlay (1863-1870). Refactor these into ONE shared `strategy_profile(Strategy)` returning weights {ovr_w, pot_w, youth_w, max_vet_age, min_pot, timeline_w, contract_len_delta, risk_sign} so a single table governs all decision sites and the buy-vs-develop / contract-length / target-age behavior becomes coherent and tunable in one place.

maps.component: AI player-evaluation / signing scorer across Team.cpp (FA-fill, scout, upgrade scan, import appetite, market value, timeline fit) and GameManager.cpp (snake-draft score_candidate, B8 market-competition bidding)
current_behavior: There are FOUR DISTINCT, NON-SHARED scoring paths that pick players, each with its own formula: (1) GameManager.cpp:2996 score_candidate (initial snake draft), (2) Team.cpp:826 CandidateRanker + Team.cpp:905 fill_one composite (offseason auto_fill_roster / cascade-fill), (3) Team.cpp:765 scout_top_fas (ScoutPrefs-weighted), (4) Team.cpp:1702 Stage-2 upgrade scan (cut-and-replace). The B8 path (GameManager.cpp:2041) does NOT score the PLAYER at all — it scores which RIVAL TEAM gets to bid.

Snake-draft score_candidate (GameManager.cpp:3012-3032): strategy-driven base. Contender/WinNow = ovr*1.4 + career_matches*0.10; Rebuild/DevFocus = potential*1.2 + max(0,25-age)*6.0; Bridge = ovr*0.9 + potential*0.5; BudgetRoster = ovr*0.7 - amount_k*0.3; TalentFarm = (potential-ovr)*5.0 + max(0,24-age)*5.0. So AGE/POTENTIAL-vs-strategy IS weighted here, but only via these fixed per-strategy formulas.

Snake-draft role-need (GameManager.cpp:3034-3039): need = target_comp.need[role] - have; if need>0 add flat +60.0, else flat -30.0. Binary gate, no magnitude scaling (being 3 short of a role == being 1 short).

Snake-draft also adds: late-round IGL +200 (3043), import-quality penalty -50 if cross-region ovr<60 (3048), org-memory nudges +25/-15/-30/+20 and financial_discipline -0.20*amount_k (3057-3064), timeline_fit_score*0.60 (3071), and archetype consistency risk_bias +-120/+-80 (3078-3088).

auto_fill fill_one composite (Team.cpp:962-1010): CandidateRanker.total = comp_fit_score*0.50 + strat_score*0.35 + pers_score*0.15 (Team.cpp:868-872). Then + 0.40*timeline_fit (975) + risk_bias (995) + 8.0*total_pos_chem chemistry-reunite (1008). strat_score (831-852) mirrors snake-draft strategy formulas but adds avg_match_rating (form) for Contender.

comp_fit_score (Team.cpp:31-61) is the dominant 50% term in fill_one: pure attribute-weighted sum keyed to the team's CompTag (e.g. DoubleDuelist wants Aim*1.4+Entry*1.4...). This is a COMP-STYLE fit, NOT a role-slot need check.

fill_one role handling (Team.cpp:939-943): role is a hard PRE-FILTER (valid pool reduced to wanted_role if any exist), not a scored term. Need MAGNITUDE never enters the score; the caller (auto_fill_roster) picks the fill order by slot.

scout_top_fas (Team.cpp:785-797): role is a hard filter (line 779), then score = ScoutPrefs-weighted sum of youth/experience/mechanics/smarts/aggressiveness/clutch/potential/flexibility. ScoutPrefs are RANDOM per team (random_scout_prefs, Team.cpp:62-72). Affordability gate get_transfer_value()<=budget (794). No upgrade-vs-incumbent, no OVR term at all.

Stage-2 upgrade scan (Team.cpp:1702-1792): the ONLY genuinely upgrade-gated path. starter_value (1711-1717) = ovr + youth bonus (age<=24: +(pot-ovr)*0.30) - age penalty (age>=30: -5) + avg_match_rating*8 (form). Upgrade requires delta = up_val - inc_val >= +4.0 (1738,1752), same role only (1742), incumbent not protected (rating<1.10 at 1731, not young-stud at 1732), cost <= 40% budget (1758), candidate accepts (1763). Capped at kMaxUpgradesPerPass=1 (1724).

B8 market competition (GameManager.cpp:2061-2124): only triggers for walked stars ovr>=75 (2045). Picks rival TEAMS by composite = team_import_appetite + budget/4M + prestige/200 (2078-2080), gated by hard positional-need check target_comp.need[role] > have (2074) and budget floor (2065). Then each of top-3 teams bids; winner = max willingness*amount_k (2117). The player is NOT scored/ranked against alternatives — it's a pure poach-auction for one named star.

team_import_appetite (Team.cpp:232-370): produces a 0..0.28 probability, NOT a player score. Sums budget(<=.10)+prestige(<=.08)+strategy(<=.06)+intl(.05)+role-scarcity(<=.06)+coach(.02)+memory(+-.04)+window(+-.05), times region_mult. Used only as a per-pick coin-flip gate on whether imports are even considered (GameManager.cpp:2988, Team.cpp:914).
market_value_estimate (Team.cpp:2427-2433): trivially just gen_contract(...).amount_k with no randomization. Used in B8 as a budget floor (GameManager.cpp:2048), not in any player ranking.

timeline_fit_score (Team.cpp:2235-2304): current_value(ovr)*1.0 + future_value((pot-ovr)*youth_factor)*0.50 + contract_efficiency*0.40 + timeline_fit-table*0.80. The piecewise table (2282-2294) keys age-band x ovr/pot-band x TeamWindow. This is the closest thing to FM-style strategy/window fit, but it's only a 0.40-0.60 weighted ADD-ON in two of the four paths and absent from scout_top_fas and the upgrade scan.

gaps: ROLE NEED is binary, never magnitude-gated. Snake-draft uses a flat +60/-30 (GameManager.cpp:3038-3039); fill_one and scout_top_fas treat role as a hard pre-filter only. FM weights need by DEPTH at position (starter gap vs 3rd-string), squad-status promise, and how far below the depth chart the player would sit. Here a team 3 short at a role values a candidate identically to one 1 short.

UPGRADE-over-incumbent exists in exactly ONE path (Team.cpp:1702 Stage-2) and is a crude fixed +4 OVR delta on a hand-rolled starter_value, capped at 1 per offseason. The snake-draft, scout_top_fas, fill_one, and B8 paths never compare a candidate to who they'd replace — they sign the highest absolute score even if the incumbent is better. FM always scores acquisition relative to the current depth chart at that slot.

No unified scorer: four divergent formulas (snake-draft strategy switch, CandidateRanker 50/35/15, ScoutPrefs random weights, starter_value) mean a player's desirability is path-dependent and incoherent. comp_fit_score (50% of fill_one) is attribute-style fit, NOT positional/role-need fit, so the dominant term doesn't even encode need.

BUDGET enters only as hard affordability gates (transfer_value<=budget, cost<=40%/20% of budget) and a weak linear penalty in two strategies (BudgetRoster -amount_k*0.3/0.5, financial_discipline -0.20*amount_k). There is no value-for-money / cost-per-OVR / cap-efficiency ranking term that trades a slightly-worse cheaper player against a marginally-better expensive one (FM's wage-budget and value-for-money logic). contract_efficiency exists only inside timeline_fit_score (Team.cpp:2298) as a 0.40-weighted sub-term, absent from scout and upgrade paths.

STRATEGY-FIT is shallow and duplicated: strategy only swaps which of ~6 hardcoded formulas runs (ovr*1.4 vs potential*1.5 etc.). timeline_fit_score adds a real window x age table but is weighted only 0.40-0.60 and is entirely missing from scout_top_fas and the Stage-2 upgrade scan, so two of four signing paths ignore championship-window/timeline coherence completely.

ROLE_FIT (role_fit_score / off-role penalty) exists for agent assignment (Team.cpp:1317,1494 role_fit_penalty_mult) but is NOT used in any signing scorer — a player is only ever evaluated on primary_role via a hard equality filter, so off-role flexibility / secondary-role coverage never influences who gets signed.

AGE/POTENTIAL vs strategy is present but coarse: handled by linear max(0,25-age) ramps and strategy switches, plus the timeline table. No proper peak-age curve, no development-trajectory / regen projection, no 'win-now vs build' explicit toggle beyond the strategy enum. potential is treated as a flat scalar with no risk/variance on whether it's reached (the consistency_mod risk_bias is the only nod and it's archetype-based, not potential-confidence based).

B8 never ranks the acquired player against the buyer's other options — it's a single-star poach auction. A team with budget+need will bid on the one walking star even if a cheaper, better-fitting FA exists; there's no comparative shortlist.

scout_top_fas uses RANDOM per-team ScoutPrefs (Team.cpp:62) with no OVR/role-need/budget-efficiency anchoring, so this path can sign clearly inferior players that merely match a team's random scouting bias; it's effectively noise, not GM judgement.
overhaul_notes: Unify on ONE player-evaluation function, e.g. double evaluate_signing(const Team& t, const Player& p, Role slot, const Player* incumbent), and have all four callers (snake-draft score_candidate GameManager.cpp:2996, fill_one Team.cpp:905, scout_top_fas Team.cpp:765, Stage-2 upgrade Team.cpp:1726, and B8's player side GameManager.cpp:2099) route through it. Today they diverge — collapsing them is the single biggest depth win.

Make need magnitude-aware: replace the flat +60/-30 (GameManager.cpp:3038) and the hard role pre-filters (Team.cpp:941,779) with a depth-chart score = f(target_comp.need[role] - have, current best-at-role OVR, squad-status). Pass the depth gap as a multiplier, not a binary.

Make upgrade-gating universal: generalize starter_value (Team.cpp:1711) into the unified scorer so EVERY path scores (candidate_value - incumbent_at_slot_value) rather than absolute value. Keep the +4 threshold configurable; remove the 1-per-offseason cap dependency by folding it into a global signings budget instead.

Add a real value-for-money term: surface contract_efficiency (currently buried in timeline_fit_score Team.cpp:2298) as a first-class weighted component (perceived_value - amount_k, or OVR-per-dollar) in the unified scorer, and fold market_value_estimate (Team.cpp:2427) in as the baseline so over/under-pays are scored everywhere, not just gated.

Promote timeline_fit_score (Team.cpp:2235) to a mandatory component of the unified scorer with a tunable weight, so window/age/potential coherence applies to scout_top_fas and upgrades too. The piecewise table is already a good FM-style spine — extend its bands rather than rebuild.

Replace random ScoutPrefs (Team.cpp:62) influence in scout_top_fas with prefs that BIAS a sane base score (OVR + need + value) rather than BEING the whole score; keep prefs as a flavour overlay (+-10%) like personality is in CandidateRanker.

Introduce role_fit_score into signing (it already exists for agent assignment, Team.cpp:role_fit_penalty_mult) so secondary-role/flex coverage adds value — lets a team value a flex player filling two thin slots, an FM-style versatility premium.

Keep team_import_appetite (Team.cpp:232) as the GATE it is, but feed its role-scarcity and window terms into the unified need/strategy score so the import decision and the player score share inputs instead of being computed twice.

Constraints to respect: sign_player runs IGL/Flex invariants (don't bypass it); scoring must stay pure/O(1) like timeline_fit_score for the snake-draft inner loop over the whole FA pool (GameManager.cpp:3107); roster cap 5 + max_imports + per-role stacking guards (Team.cpp:1832) are hard rules the scorer should respect via filters, not soft-score around.

maps.component: Starting-5 selection & AI benching (Valosim/cpp — Team roster ordering, swap_roster_slots, auto_fill_roster, enforce_one_igl/flex, AI roster passes)
current_behavior: Starting 5 == roster[0..4], pure POSITION/sign-order. `build_round_selection` fields exactly the first 5 vector slots: `std::vector<PlayerPtr> players(roster.begin(), roster.begin() + 5);` (Team.cpp:1347) and the user-agent-override path `std::vector<PlayerPtr> ps(roster.begin(), roster.begin() + 5);` (Team.cpp:1305). There is NO sort by OVR/form anywhere before fielding — index position alone decides who starts.

auto_fill_roster (Team.cpp:896) builds the starting 5 by APPEND ORDER into empty slots: slot1 Duelist, slot2 Controller, slot3 Initiator, slot4 Sentinel (Team.cpp:1072-1075), then a flexible 5th cascade (flex player -> 2nd Initiator -> 2nd Controller -> best remaining FA) (Team.cpp:1078-1086). Whoever is signed first lands in the lower index and thus starts. It fills vacancies only (`if (roster.size() >= 5) return false;` Team.cpp:907) — it never reorders or demotes an existing starter.

fill_one ranks FA CANDIDATES by a composite (CandidateRanker + timeline_fit + risk_bias + chemistry, Team.cpp:962-1043) — so signing is merit-based, but that merit only orders who gets SIGNED, not which rostered player STARTS. Once signed, push order is positional.

swap_roster_slots (Team.cpp:732-750, declared Team.h:293) is the ONLY starter<->bench mutator: validates both players exist on opposite sides of the `line = min(5, roster.size())` bench boundary (Team.cpp:740-743), does `std::iter_swap` (Team.cpp:744), then re-runs enforce_one_igl()/enforce_one_flex() (Team.cpp:748-749). Benched player stays rostered and PAID. It is called from exactly ONE place: the user GUI Full-Roster tab (gui_main.cpp:6076, `t->swap_roster_slots(sw.first, sw.second)`). No AI code path calls it.

enforce_one_igl (Team.cpp:538-658) and enforce_one_flex (Team.cpp:660-712) operate ONLY on `starters = min(5, roster.size())` (Team.cpp:540, 669). They flip is_igl/is_flex overlay flags and apply/revert the IGL stat shift — they do NOT move players between starter and bench indices. They cannot bench an underperformer; they only fix leadership/flex legality of whoever already occupies roster[0..4].

AI 'benching' mid-season is actually a CUT+SIGN, not a bench: run_mid_season_replacements (GameManager.cpp:1091-1266) finds the worst starter under a 0.90 rating ceiling (GameManager.cpp:1108-1125), then `t->release_player(worst)` + `t->sign_player(best_replacement,...)` (GameManager.cpp:1247-1253). The released player leaves the roster entirely (goes to FA pool) — he is NOT retained on the bench. The UI even says 'benches' (GameManager.cpp:1227) but the mechanic is a release.

ai_manage_roster (Team.cpp:1511-1552, AI year-end) only DROPS expired/poor players (`release_player`) then `auto_fill_roster` to refill (Team.cpp:1550-1551). No reorder, no bench. Cut gate: career_matches>10 && avg_match_rating()<threshold && potential<65 (Team.cpp:1525-1527).

ai_full_offseason_pass (Team.cpp:1631-1878, user-team OFFSEASON fallback only) does CUT-AND-REPLACE of starters (Stage 2, Team.cpp:1726-1792: release incumbent, sign +4-OVR FA upgrade), cascade-fill (Stage 3), and bench-DEPTH SIGNING (Stage 4, Team.cpp:1822-1865 signs an extra FA into slot 6). It never benches an existing underperforming starter behind an existing rostered reserve — every 'upgrade' is an external FA cut-and-replace, never an internal reorder via swap_roster_slots.

release_player (Team.cpp:714-730) erases the player; erase() can shift a bench player up into the starting 5, so it re-runs enforce_one_igl/flex (Team.cpp:727-728). This is the only way a reserve 'promotes' under AI control — and only as a side effect of a CUT, never a merit-based bench decision.
key_symbols: Team::swap_roster_slots(const PlayerPtr& benchee, const PlayerPtr& starter) -> bool — Team.cpp:732 / Team.h:293. THE bench mutator. iter_swap + enforce_one_igl/flex. Currently only called at gui_main.cpp:6076.
Team::auto_fill_roster(std::vector<PlayerPtr>& free_agents, int current_year=0) — Team.cpp:896 / Team.h:364. Vacancy-fill by D/C/I/S + flex-5th append order; never reorders existing starters.
Team::enforce_one_igl() — Team.cpp:538 / Team.h:331. Overlay-flag fixer on roster[0..4]; no index movement.
Team::enforce_one_flex() — Team.cpp:660 / Team.h:354. Overlay-flag fixer on roster[0..4]; insert-only; no index movement.
Team::ai_manage_roster(std::vector<PlayerPtr>&, int current_year) — Team.cpp:1511 / Team.h:395. AI year-end: drop+refill only.
Team::ai_full_offseason_pass(std::vector<PlayerPtr>&, int, std::vector<std::string>& log) — Team.cpp:1631 / Team.h:434. User-team offseason: cut-and-replace + bench-depth sign; no internal bench.
GameManager::run_mid_season_replacements(std::vector<std::string>& log) — GameManager.cpp:1091. AI mid-season: release worst starter + sign FA (cut, not bench).
Team::build_round_selection(...) — Team.cpp:1347 fields roster.begin()..begin()+5 (positional starters). The consumer that makes roster index == 'starting 5' load-bearing.
starter_value lambda — Team.cpp:1711-1717 (ovr + youth potential + form*8). Only existing form/OVR ranking heuristic; used solely to gate external upgrades, never to rank own roster for benching.
igl_candidate_score / flex_score lambdas — Team.cpp:500 / Team.cpp:681. Merit scorers, but only for overlay election, not for starter selection.
gaps: No merit-based starting-5 selection exists. The user's claim is CONFIRMED: the AI never benches an underperformer in favor of a better rostered reserve. Starting 5 = sign order (roster[0..4]), set by auto_fill_roster append order (Team.cpp:1072-1086), never re-sorted by OVR or form. Evidence: zero std::sort over `roster` in the codebase (only std::find at Team.cpp:716, 2392); build_round_selection fields the literal first 5 (Team.cpp:1347).

swap_roster_slots, the correct mutator for benching, has exactly ONE caller and it is the human UI (gui_main.cpp:6076). No AI/GameManager/Team code calls it. So a rostered 6th man who is better than a slumping starter will NEVER take the start under AI control.

Mid-season 'benching' is mislabeled: run_mid_season_replacements RELEASES the underperformer (GameManager.cpp:1247) instead of benching him to retain depth — and it requires an EXTERNAL FA upgrade (fa->ovr() >= worst->ovr(), GameManager.cpp:1202) + role_pool_count>=4 (GameManager.cpp:1211). If the better option is already on your own bench, this code can't use it (it only scans the FA pool, GameManager.cpp:1183).

ai_full_offseason_pass Stage 4 SIGNS a bench piece (Team.cpp:1822) but there is no follow-up pass that would ever start that bench piece over a weak starter — the new signing is appended (high index) and stays benched permanently unless the human swaps it.

Consequence vs FM depth: in Football Manager the manager picks a best XI by form/fit each match and rotates; here the 'XI' is frozen at whatever order players were signed in, and only changes when someone is CUT. A young high-OVR signing that lands at roster[5] never displaces a declining roster[2] starter automatically.

enforce_one_igl/flex can mask the problem: they make roster[0..4] LEGAL (1 IGL, >=1 flex) but say nothing about whether those 5 are the BEST 5 — they will happily certify a starting 5 containing a 0.70-rating slumping starter while a 1.10-rating reserve sits at index 6.
overhaul_notes: Add a new merit pass, e.g. `Team::optimize_starting_five(int current_year)`, whose ONLY mutator is swap_roster_slots (so IGL/flex re-enforcement and the 'still-paid bench' semantics come for free). For each role-comparable bench player (roster[5..]) vs the weakest same-role starter, compute a fit score and swap_roster_slots when the reserve clearly beats the starter. Reuse the existing starter_value lambda (Team.cpp:1711) — promote it to a private member/free function so both ai_full_offseason_pass and the new pass share one 'how good is this guy right now' scorer (ovr + youth potential*0.30 + avg_match_rating*8).

Run it in BOTH places the user asked: (1) OFFSEASON — call after Stage 3 cascade-fill / Stage 4 bench-depth inside ai_full_offseason_pass (Team.cpp:~1865, before the Stage 5 report) AND inside ai_manage_roster (Team.cpp:1551, right after auto_fill_roster) so AI teams also re-optimize at year-end. (2) MID-SEASON — call inside run_mid_season_replacements (GameManager.cpp, after the cut/replace loop body per team, ~GameManager.cpp:1256) BEFORE deciding to cut: prefer an internal bench swap over an external FA release when a rostered reserve already beats the worst starter. This stops the engine from releasing depth it could have benched.

Guardrails to keep it FM-realistic, not thrash: (a) hysteresis — only swap when reserve_score - starter_score exceeds a margin (e.g. +3 to +4, mirror the +4 OVR threshold at Team.cpp:1738) so you don't flip-flop weekly; (b) role-match — only swap players whose primary_role matches (or whose comp slot matches) so you don't break the D/C/I/S+flex shape that auto_fill built; (c) prospect protection — never bench a young high-potential starter who needs reps (mirror is_prospect age<=21 && potential>=75, GameManager.cpp:1119); (d) sample gate — require season_matches>=6 before trusting form (mirror GameManager.cpp:1114).

swap_roster_slots is already the correct, safe mutator — it validates the bench line (Team.cpp:740-743), preserves contracts/pay, and re-runs enforce_one_igl/enforce_one_flex (Team.cpp:748-749). The overhaul should NOT introduce a parallel reorder path; route every AI bench decision through it so the starting-5 legality invariant is automatically maintained, exactly as the GUI path does.

Note an ordering subtlety: enforce_one_igl applies/reverts a stat shift on IGL change. Calling swap_roster_slots repeatedly is safe (it's idempotent re: flags), but a batch optimizer should compute all desired swaps against a snapshot first, then apply them, to avoid mid-loop index/flag churn (same pattern the GUI uses: it collects `to_swap` then applies after the render loop, gui_main.cpp:6075).

Decide whether AI teams should bench at all or only the user team: today ai_manage_roster (AI) is intentionally the shallow drop+refill path and ai_full_offseason_pass (deep) is user-team-only per its Team.h:418-436 doc. If the desired behavior is 'all teams pick a merit-based best 5', the new pass must be wired into the AI year-end path (ai_manage_roster) and the AI mid-season path (run_mid_season_replacements), not just the user offseason fallback, otherwise only the human's club benches and AI clubs stay frozen at sign order.

maps.component: Mid-season AI roster management (GameManager::run_mid_season_replacements + advance_day monthly tick)
current_behavior: IN-SEASON roster management EXISTS today (not only year-end). The 30-day tick inside advance_day fires run_monthly_progression then run_mid_season_replacements then update_mvp_race: C:/Users/fulls/Desktop/Valosim/cpp/src/GameManager.cpp:1045-1052 (`if (days_since_progression >= 30) { ... run_mid_season_replacements(log); ... }`).

run_mid_season_replacements (GameManager.cpp:1091-1266) is the ONLY in-season roster mutator. It is CUT-AND-REPLACE only: for each tier-1 league team with a head_coach, it finds the single worst starter (roster[0..4]) under a 0.90 season-avg-rating ceiling (GameManager.cpp:1108-1125), rolls a personality/team-success/window/contract-gated cut_chance (GameManager.cpp:1131-1161), scans the regional solo-Q ladder for a same-role FA upgrade that meets/beats the cut player's OVR (GameManager.cpp:1183-1207), and if found executes t->release_player(worst) + t->sign_player(replacement) (GameManager.cpp:1247-1255).

The day loop itself never touches rosters: play_stage_round (declared GameManager.h:350) and play_tournament_round (GameManager.h:357) only simulate matches and record W/L — no sign/cut/bench/trade anywhere in them.

There is NO benching-to-bench (swap starter with roster[5..6]), NO trade between teams, NO emergency/injury replacement, and NO tier-2-riser or top-solo-Q-FA 'fits a need' promotion logic anywhere in-season. The existing path only releases to FA and signs an FA of the SAME role that is already an OVR upgrade.

The user team is NOT excluded from run_mid_season_replacements — the loop at GameManager.cpp:1101-1103 iterates every team in `leagues` gated only on `t->head_coach`, with no `t == user_team` skip and no `user_auto_manage` check. So the user's own roster can already be auto-cut mid-season regardless of the auto-manage opt-in (contrast the year-end fallback which is gated on roster<5 / user_auto_manage at GameManager.cpp:917-940 and the header note GameManager.h:145-147).

Mid-season only looks at the FA subset of the regional solo-Q ladder (`fa->team_name != "Free Agent"` skip at GameManager.cpp:1185). It never reads tier_leagues_ (tier-2 rostered risers) and never reads other regions' ladders beyond the import-appetite gate.

gaps: No benching-to-bench: the user explicitly wants AI to BENCH a player mid-season for a well-performing alternative, but the only mid-season action is release-to-FA + sign-an-FA. Team::swap_roster_slots (Team.cpp:732) is the purpose-built never-free-safe bench mutator and is wired into the GUI (per MEMORY 'BENCHING' batch) but is NEVER invoked by the engine/AI. So 'bench for a better fit' literally cannot happen via AI today.

No tier-2-riser sourcing: candidate scan only considers solo-Q-ladder FAs (GameManager.cpp:1183-1185 requires team_name=="Free Agent"). Well-performing tier-2 ROSTERED players (the user's 'well-performing tier-2 players') are in tier_leagues_[region][1..] and are completely invisible to the mid-season logic — there is no cross-tier signing path in-season (cross-tier moves only happen via year-end promotion/relegation brackets).

'Fits a need' is shallow: the replacement must be the EXACT same primary_role as the cut player (GameManager.cpp:1186) and simply OVR>=worst (GameManager.cpp:1202). There is no notion of a roster NEED (missing role / weak role / comp gap / IGL or Flex hole) driving the search — it only ever upgrades the position it just vacated. scout_top_fas (Team.cpp:765) already computes role NEED from target_comp.need but is not used mid-season.

No recent-form signal: the cut trigger uses avg_match_rating() which is the WHOLE-season average (season_rating_total/season_matches, Player.h:481-482). There is no rolling 'last N matches' form window, so a player on a recent hot or cold streak is judged on cumulative season rating — the comment at GameManager.cpp:1098-1099 claims it weighs 'recent vs long-term form' but no such recent metric exists in the data model.

No top-solo-Q-FA emphasis: the scan ranks FAs by ovr - demand/50 (GameManager.cpp:1205); it does not surface a climbing solo-Q phenom (peak_mmr / solo_mmr in Player.h:516-517 are unused here). The user's 'top solo-Q FAs' angle is not modeled.

User team handled wrong for this feature: run_mid_season_replacements currently fires on the user team unconditionally (no user_team skip at GameManager.cpp:1101). For an opt-in AI-GM feature it should be gated on user_auto_manage (GameManager.h:147) like the year-end pass is, otherwise a hands-on player gets their roster changed under them.
No trade / emergency replacement: there is zero team-to-team trade machinery and no injury/unavailability emergency-replacement path anywhere in the codebase.
overhaul_notes: Hook location is already correct and should be reused: extend the existing monthly tick at GameManager.cpp:1045-1052. A periodic (e.g. monthly) AI roster-review belongs INSIDE or right after run_mid_season_replacements, on the same `days_since_progression >= 30` cadence (counter GameManager.h:153, reset at GameManager.cpp:1050). It already runs AFTER run_monthly_progression so ratings/aging for the month are settled before review.

Data the review should read (all already available): per-player season form via Player::avg_match_rating()/season_matches (Player.h:481,561); team standings via Team::wins/losses + phase_wins/phase_losses (Team.h:271-272) and the net_w team-success modifier already computed at GameManager.cpp:1130-1131; FA pool + top solo-Q via SoloQEngine::global_ladder() (SoloQ.h:29) filtered on team_name=="Free Agent" for FAs (and peak_mmr/solo_mmr Player.h:516-517 for solo-Q risers); tier-2 risers via tier_leagues_[region][1] teams' rosters (GameManager.h:176, league_at GameManager.h:190); roster NEED via Team::target_comp.need[role] vs current role counts (the pattern is in scout_top_fas Team.cpp:766-772); existing power rankings (compute_power_rankings GameManager.h:322) for standings context.

Never-free-safe mutators to call (NONE destroy a Player/Team — they only reparent shared_ptrs, preserving the 'never free anything' invariant): for BENCH-for-a-better-fit use Team::swap_roster_slots(benchee, starter) (Team.cpp:732) — the benched player stays rostered and PAID; for cut/sign keep Team::release_player (Team.cpp:714, player goes to FA, object survives) + Team::sign_player (Team.cpp:407/414) + Team::resign_player (in-place, Team.h:301). All four already re-run enforce_one_igl/enforce_one_flex so the starting 5 stays legal. Respect sign_player's roster.size()>=7 guard (Team.cpp:417) and the import cap (config().max_imports, GameManager.cpp:1195).

Signing a tier-2 ROSTERED riser requires a cross-tier move that is currently UNMODELED in-season: the riser's current tier-2 team must release_player first (or a transfer-fee path added), then user/AI team sign_player. There is no helper for this today — apply_promotion/apply_relegation (GameManager.h:423-424) move whole TEAMS between tiers, not individual players. A new 'poach from lower tier' mutator (release from tier-2 team + sign to tier-1 team) would be needed; it is never-free-safe as long as it only release+sign (no erase from ladder).

User-team gating: gate any AI-driven mid-season bench/sign on the user team behind `user_auto_manage` (GameManager.h:147) — mirror the year-end fallback's opt-in at GameManager.cpp:917 — so a hands-on player isn't auto-managed. Today's run_mid_season_replacements lacks this skip (GameManager.cpp:1101); add `if (t == user_team && !user_auto_manage) continue;`.

Add a recent-form window to the data model: there is no rolling last-N-matches rating today. Either add a small ring buffer of recent per-match ratings to Player (alongside season_rating_total Player.h:482) populated at the Match.cpp end-of-match block, or derive form from the existing Player::history (MatchHistoryEntry vector, Player.h:552). The 'fit a need' search should combine: roster role gap (target_comp.need) x candidate recent form x OVR/upgrade delta x affordability (budget/4 cap pattern at GameManager.cpp:1204).

Surface decisions via the existing news pipeline: reuse push_news with category "Roster Move" exactly as the current cut path does (GameManager.cpp:1222-1245) so benchings/signings appear in the Breaking-News feed; add distinct headlines for BENCH vs CUT vs PROMOTE-FROM-TIER-2 so the user sees the reasoning.

maps.component: Coach system (CoachPersonality archetypes) and its influence on player development vs. AI roster management

Coaches are stored on the team as `CoachPtr head_coach;` at include/Team.h:270. One coach per team; default-null (Free Agent). Generated/assigned in GameManager.cpp at world init (140-142), year-end aging/refresh (1546-1558, 2250-2260), and league spawn (4571-4573, 4862-4866).

Coach archetype EXISTS as 16 personalities (Coach.h:16-33): Disciplinarian, PlayerFocused, TacticalGenius, DevelopmentCoach, AggressiveRiskTaker, StructuredMacro, EmotionDriven, AnalyticsHeavy, VeteranMentor, HarshRebuilder, Motivator, Pragmatist, Innovator, DefensiveAnchor, EntryLover, BudgetBalancer. PLAYER DEVELOPMENT — coach affects growth. gaps: Roster construction is driven entirely by Team::Strategy (an OBJECTIVE classifier from budget/ovr/age/potential at Team.cpp:204-229) and TeamWindow — coach archetype is invisible to it. A DevelopmentCoach on a cash-rich, high-OVR team still gets classified WinNow and signs proven veterans (Team.cpp:223, CandidateRanker strat_score 834-841). The archetype the UI advertises (Coach.cpp:62 'Builds talent from rookies up') has zero effect on signings.

CandidateRanker (Team.cpp:826-873) — the core FA-signing scorer used by auto_fill_roster (Team.cpp:962) — has NO coach field. It only knows comp/Team::Strategy/Team::Personality. So 'a dev coach prioritizes youth' / 'a win-now coach demands proven stars' is currently impossible at the signing layer.

The two example asks map cleanly onto existing archetypes but are unused for signing: DevelopmentCoach/BudgetBalancer/VeteranMentor (youth/in-house/vets) and Pragmatist/AggressiveRiskTaker (wins-now/proven). Today only their replacement-aggressiveness (Coach.cpp:79-99) matters, which is a CUT cadence, not a SIGNING philosophy.

team_eval_score (GameManager.cpp:1804-1873, the re-sign keep/walk gate) and team_import_appetite (Team.cpp:232-355) both branch on Strategy/Window but never read head_coach — so coach archetype can't make a VeteranMentor keep an aging core or a DevelopmentCoach let vets walk.

Mid-season replacement (the one coach-gated path) only consults personality for the CUT probability (GameManager.cpp:1105) — the replacement search at 1183-1207 ignores archetype, so even a DevelopmentCoach replaces with the highest-OVR FA rather than a young prospect.

Coach.cpp's stat biases hint at intended archetype identity (DevelopmentCoach high development, Pragmatist balanced) but nothing converts those into roster preferences; the development stat only feeds dev_chance_mult (growth), not signing logic.
decide_contract_years (Team.cpp:1554) sets contract length with no coach input — a win-now coach should hand out short/expensive deals, a dev coach longer cheap ones; currently archetype-blind.
overhaul_notes: Add a coach->roster-preference accessor on Coach (e.g. in Coach.cpp next to personality_replacement_aggressiveness, Coach.cpp:79-99) returning weights: youth_bias, proven_bias, budget_bias, import_bias, vet_retention_bias, risk_tolerance. This becomes the single source the roster AI reads, so archetype maps to signing philosophy.

Primary gate point #1: inject coach into CandidateRanker (Team.cpp:826-873). Add a `const Coach* coach` field and a `coach_score(p)` term in total() (Team.cpp:868-872), and pass head_coach at the construction site Team.cpp:962. This is THE chokepoint for 'who do we sign' in auto_fill_roster.

Primary gate point #2: have coach archetype bias/override Team::Strategy. Cleanest is to feed coach into classify_team_strategy (Team.cpp:204-229) or the inertia layer commit_strategy_with_inertia (Team.cpp ~2124) so a DevelopmentCoach nudges toward DevelopmentFocus/Rebuilding and a Pragmatist/AggressiveRiskTaker toward Contender/WinNow even when raw budget/ovr would say otherwise. Note strategy is recomputed at GameManager.cpp:157,180,2302,4576,4605,4876 — gate once in the classifier so all sites inherit it.

Gate point #3: team_eval_score re-sign keep/walk (GameManager.cpp:1804-1873). Add coach branches alongside the existing Strategy/Window blocks (1862-1870): VeteranMentor +score for age>=28, DevelopmentCoach/HarshRebuilder -score for vets / +score for youth. head_coach is reachable via the team `t` already in scope.

Gate point #4: team_import_appetite (Team.cpp:232-355) — add a coach term beside the strategy/window terms (262-355): BudgetBalancer/DevelopmentCoach lower import appetite (develop in-house), AggressiveRiskTaker/Pragmatist raise it.

Gate point #5: decide_contract_years (Team.cpp:1554) and the risk_bias block in auto_fill_roster (Team.cpp:979-995) — let coach risk_tolerance replace/augment the strategy-only branch so AggressiveRiskTaker gambles on volatile high-ceilings regardless of strategy.

Gate point #6 (already partly wired): run_mid_season_replacements (GameManager.cpp:1183-1207) — make the replacement SEARCH archetype-aware (DevelopmentCoach prefers young/high-potential replacement, Pragmatist prefers proven OVR), not just the cut probability.

Constraints: one coach per team (Team.h:270) and head_coach can be null — every new gate must null-check (mirror GameManager.cpp:1103 `if (!t->head_coach) continue` and the 0.95 no-coach default at Player.cpp:1722). User team is excluded from AI roster moves (GameManager.cpp comment line 20, OFFSEASON), so coach-gating must respect that exclusion. Personality is hidden in UI by default (Coach.h:14) — keep it observation-only; don't surface archetype labels in non-godmode roster reasoning. Strategy has inertia (previous_strategy, Team.h:138-146); coach bias should feed the suggested strategy, not bypass the inertia smoothing.

maps.component: Talent Pipeline / Scouting & Signing (tier-2 risers + top solo-Q FAs into tier-1 AI rosters)
current_behavior: solo_qs is the per-region persistent player registry: GameManager holds `std::map<std::string, SoloQEnginePtr> solo_qs` keyed by region; the ladder is `SoloQEngine::ladder_` exposed via `global_ladder()` (SoloQ.h:29-34). EVERY player (tier-1 pros, tier-2 pros, and unsigned FAs) lives on this ladder for life — sign/release never add or remove ladder entries (Team.cpp:407-466 sign_player, 714-730 release_player only touch `roster` + `team_name`). This is the never-free invariant (gui_main.cpp:1396).

The FA pool handed to every AI signing routine IS the region's full solo_q ladder, then filtered to `team_name == "Free Agent"`. Year-end tier-1 GM pass builds it as `auto fa_pool = solo_qs[kv.first]->global_ladder();` and calls `t->ai_full_offseason_pass(fa_pool, year + 1, log)` (GameManager.cpp:2263, 2284).

ai_full_offseason_pass scans candidates ONLY via `fa->team_name != "Free Agent"` guards (Team.cpp:1741 upgrade scan, 1827 bench scan) and through auto_fill_roster's `collect_free_agents` which filters `team_name == "Free Agent"` (Team.cpp:813-821). A rostered tier-2 standout has team_name == his tier-2 club, so he is structurally invisible to every tier-1 scan.
scout_top_fas (Team.cpp:765-808) — the role-need scout used at world init — also hard-filters `p->team_name != "Free Agent"` (Team.cpp:779). Same blind spot.
Mid-season replacement scan (GameManager.cpp:1163-1207) pulls from `solo_qs[t->region]->global_ladder()` but again filters `fa->team_name != "Free Agent"` (line 1185). Only true FAs are reachable.

Tier-2 players ARE tracked for performance identically to tier-1: tier-2 league matches run through sim_series_returning_all_matches with league_play=true (play_tier_stage_round, GameManager.cpp:4640-4660), so they accrue career_rating_total/career_matches (-> avg_match_rating(), Player.cpp:213-215) and season_rating_total/season_matches (Player.h:481-482). Tier-2 team standing is `team->wins` (used for Ascension seeding, GameManager.cpp:4756). solo_mmr/peak_mmr are tracked for ALL ladder players (SoloQ.cpp simulate_ranked_day:366-379), including FAs.

The ONLY current paths a tier-2 player reaches tier-1: (a) whole-team Ascension promotion via the relegation gauntlet (resolve_promotion_relegation + apply_promotion, GameManager.cpp:4730-4813, 4690-4702) — promotes the TEAM, not the individual; or (b) the player is first released to Free Agent (run_lower_tier_offseason drops, GameManager.cpp:4857-4860) and only THEN becomes scoutable as an ordinary FA.

The only existing 'poach a rostered player' mechanic is B8 market competition for expiring tier-1 stars (GameManager.cpp:2033-2180): it iterates `walked` (tier-1 players who rejected re-sign), restricted to `pl_ovr >= 75.0` and same-region tier-1 rivals (`leagues.find(region)`). It never looks at tier-2 rosters or top solo-Q FAs as targets.

Player perf/scout signals — avg_match_rating() Player.cpp:213; season_rating_total/season_matches Player.h:481-482; solo_mmr/peak_mmr Player.h:516-517; discontent/transfer_requested Player.h:446-447; team standing = Team::wins
gaps: Tier-1 AI discovers talent ONLY through the Free-Agent slice of the ladder. A high-rating tier-2 starter or a 4000+ MMR climber who happens to sit on a tier-2 roster is never a scoutable target — every scan early-outs on `team_name != "Free Agent"` (Team.cpp:779, 1741, 1827; GameManager.cpp:1185). This is the core wonkiness vs the user's request.

'Top solo-Q FAs' are nominally reachable (they ARE Free Agents) but are ranked by OVR/potential/avg_match_rating only — solo_mmr/peak_mmr are NOT inputs to any scout score (scout_top_fas Team.cpp:785-793 and CandidateRanker Team.cpp:826-873 ignore solo_mmr). A genuine ranked-ladder phenom with mediocre paper OVR is invisible. Note FAs rarely play pro matches, so their avg_match_rating is thin/stale; MMR is the only fresh signal and it is unused.

generate_internal deliberately skips deep potential MC for ladder fillers (SoloQ.cpp:247-259) — so most ladder FAs have shallow `potential`. Scoring that leans on potential (TalentFarm/Rebuilding strats, bench pick) is biased against exactly the solo-Q risers the user wants surfaced, unless MMR/rating is added as a signal.

Promotion is team-granular only. There is no individual promote/sign of a tier-2 riser into a tier-1 club; the player must first be cut to FA (a full offseason of latency) before any tier-1 team can touch him (run_lower_tier_offseason:4857-4860).

B8 poaching (the one never-free cross-roster sign that exists) is gated to tier-1-only, same-region, ovr>=75 walked players (GameManager.cpp:2059-2061, 2045). It cannot be reused as-is for tier-2/solo-Q targets without widening its candidate source.
season_rating_total / avg_match_rating do accumulate for tier-2 players, so a 'tier-2 riser by rating/standing' signal already EXISTS in the data — it is simply never queried by any tier-1 scan.
overhaul_notes: Surface 'tier-2 risers': add a GameManager scout-source helper that walks `tier_leagues_[region]` tiers >= 2 rosters and yields players whose avg_match_rating (Player.cpp:213) and/or tier-2 team standing (Team::wins, GameManager.cpp:4756) clear a bar. These are rostered (team_name != Free Agent), so they must be offered as POACH targets, not appended to the team_name==Free Agent FA vector.

Surface 'top solo-Q FAs': they are already in `global_ladder()` with team_name==Free Agent; the fix is purely in SCORING — add solo_mmr/peak_mmr as a ranking input in scout_top_fas (Team.cpp:785-793) and CandidateRanker::strat_score/total (Team.cpp:831-872). Sort/take top-N FAs by solo_mmr to define the 'top solo-Q FA' shortlist a tier-1 GM evaluates against its role needs (target_comp.need vs roster have-count, the pattern at scout_top_fas:766-772).

Need-fit gate already exists and should be reused verbatim: target_comp.need[role] - have[role] (scout_top_fas:766-772) and the B8 positional-need check (GameManager.cpp:2068-2074). Only sign a riser/solo-Q FA when it fills a genuine role gap.

Never-free promote/sign path: model on B8 (GameManager.cpp:2131-2150). For a tier-2 riser the sequence is: source_tier2_team->release_player(p) (Team.cpp:714, flips team_name to Free Agent, keeps him on the ladder), then tier1_team->sign_player(p, yrs, year[+1]) (Team.cpp:414), debit budget manually (sign_player does NOT debit — see comment GameManager.cpp:2139-2142), then sync_player_ranked_region(p, tier1_region) if regions differ (GameManager.cpp:2409). No object is freed; ladder membership is preserved.

Respect existing invariants the sign path enforces: import cap config().max_imports (checked at Team.cpp:1744-1748, 2071-... ), affordability (<=40% budget for upgrade, Team.cpp:1758), acceptance gate player->accepts_resign_offer (Team.cpp:1763), and enforce_one_igl/enforce_one_flex (run automatically inside sign_player).

Best insertion point for the new behavior: inside the tier-1 year-end GM loop right where ai_full_offseason_pass is called (GameManager.cpp:2277-2284) — either (a) pre-seed the fa_pool passed in with poach-eligible tier-2/solo-Q targets flagged for the release-then-sign path, or (b) add a dedicated GameManager pass (mirroring B8) that runs after ai_full_offseason_pass and lets each tier-1 team make one poach offer to the top tier-2 riser / top solo-Q FA that fits a need. Keep it AI-only (skip user_team unless user_auto_manage, matching GameManager.cpp:2277).

Guard against draining tier-2 too hard / circular promotion: a poached tier-2 riser leaves a hole that run_lower_tier_offseason (GameManager.cpp:4815) will auto-fill from FAs the same offseason — fine, but cap poaches per tier-1 team per offseason (B8 makes one offer per player; ai_full_offseason_pass caps upgrades at kMaxUpgradesPerPass=1, Team.cpp:1724) to avoid a tier-2 talent vacuum.

maps.component: Lower-tier (Challengers/Open Circuit) AI roster management — run_lower_tier_offseason + BudgetRoster strategy + shared ai_full_offseason_pass
current_behavior: run_lower_tier_offseason iterates every region, tiers 2..tier_count, and every team in each Challengers/Open league once per year (GameManager.cpp:4815-4890).

Per tier it builds a region-local FA sub-pool filtered to team_name=='Free Agent' AND ovr() < tc.ovr_cap (T2 cap 62, T3 cap 50) so lower tiers genuinely draw from weaker talent (GameManager.cpp:4827-4834, kTiers at 4491-4495).

Pre-pass keep/drop loop: expiring players are re-signed in place only if avg_match_rating>=0.90 OR potential>=70 OR ovr>=ovr_cap-8; everyone else and retired players are dropped (GameManager.cpp:4840-4860).
Coach regen + save_history + W/L reset, then it calls the SAME t->ai_full_offseason_pass(sub, year+1, log) that tier-1 teams get (GameManager.cpp:4861-4875).
After the pass it re-classifies strategy via classify_team_strategy and runs enforce_one_igl/enforce_one_flex (GameManager.cpp:4876-4878).

ai_full_offseason_pass itself is fully shared and deep: Stage1 cut poor performers w/ chemistry retention, Stage2 per-starter value + cut-and-replace upgrade scan (capped 1/pass), Stage3 cascade auto_fill_roster for vacancies, Stage4 one optional bench piece, Stage5 report (Team.cpp:1631-1878).

auto_fill_roster enforces role-correctness by filling slots D->C->I->S then a flexible 5th (flex player / 2nd Initiator / 2nd Controller / best FA), with import caps, comp-fit ranking, and a pathological-recovery loop, then enforce_one_igl/enforce_one_flex + refresh_target_comp (Team.cpp:896-1104).
Initial lower-tier rosters are built once at world-gen via build_tier_teams + generate_lower_tiers, which also call auto_fill_roster from the ovr-capped leftover FA pool (GameManager.cpp:4550-4611).

BudgetRoster is auto-assigned when budget < 400K (Team.cpp:227) — exactly the T2/T3 budget bands (T2 200-360K, T3 90-180K, kTiers GameManager.cpp:4493-4494), so essentially ALL lower-tier teams classify as BudgetRoster.

BudgetRoster's only behavioral footprint is a cheap-value scoring tweak: CandidateRanker strat_score s = ovr*0.8 - amount_k*0.5 (Team.cpp:846-848) and the FA-scan mirror s = ovr*0.7 - amount_k*0.3 (GameManager.cpp:3026-3028); plus it re-signs through age 28 less (Player.cpp:2755), uses tier_baseline_k=300 (Team.cpp:2019) and a -0.8 prestige nudge (Team.cpp:1580).

gaps: NOT a budget auto-fill collapse — lower tiers genuinely run the identical deep ai_full_offseason_pass as tier-1 (GameManager.cpp:4875 == 2284). The shallowness is in DIFFERENTIATION, not in the code path: every Challengers/Open team gets the same generic pass with no tier-specific GM behavior.

Strategy monoculture: every lower-tier team collapses to BudgetRoster because the <400K threshold (Team.cpp:227) blankets both T2 (200-360K) and T3 (90-180K) budget bands. There is no Rebuilding/DevelopmentFocus/TalentFarm differentiation at lower tiers, so no archetypal variety in how Challengers orgs build — they all chase the same ovr*0.8 - cost*0.5 cheap-value heuristic.

BudgetRoster is the SHALLOWEST strategy profile: its only behavioral signal is two near-identical cost-penalty scoring lines (Team.cpp:846-848, GameManager.cpp:3026-3028). It carries no risk_bias branch (Team.cpp:980-995 only handles Contender/WinNow/Rebuilding/Development/TalentFarm), no timeline/youth logic — so the tier-2-riser pipeline produces undifferentiated cheap-veteran-biased squads instead of upside-hunting development orgs.

Role-correctness is structurally enforced by auto_fill_roster's D/C/I/S slot order (Team.cpp:1072-1095), BUT only when slots are VACANT (Stage3). The keep/drop pre-pass (GameManager.cpp:4844-4855) and Stage2 upgrade scan operate role-agnostically except for same-role replacement, so a lower-tier roster that drifts off-comp (e.g. two duelists, zero sentinel) via the pre-pass is never actively rebalanced — auto_fill only triggers on count<5, not on role gaps within a full 5.

Quality is capped but not floored: the ovr_cap filter (GameManager.cpp:4832) caps the FA pool ceiling, and the keep gate uses ovr_cap-8 (4847), but there is no minimum-OVR or potential floor ensuring lower-tier squads contain developmental talent worth promoting. A T3 team can fill entirely with sub-50 OVR aging FAs, which is anti-correlated with feeding believable risers.

Bench depth (Stage4) is gated on budget>=400K (Team.cpp:1822) — which NO BudgetRoster team can meet (budget<400K by definition). So lower-tier teams structurally never sign bench depth, leaving them at exactly 5 with zero developmental reserves, undercutting the riser pipeline.

Re-sign floor uses kSalaryFloorK=10K (GameManager.cpp:4851) — lower-tier economics are effectively flattened to a token floor, so no meaningful budget pressure or contention modeling distinguishes a strong Challengers org from a weak Open team.
overhaul_notes: Lower tiers already share the ai_full_offseason_pass code path, so the overhaul is additive, not a rewrite: inject tier/strategy DIFFERENTIATION rather than building a new roster routine. The single hook is run_lower_tier_offseason calling the shared pass at GameManager.cpp:4875.

Break the BudgetRoster monoculture: either lower the <400K threshold (Team.cpp:227) or add a tier-aware strategy assignment in build_tier_teams (GameManager.cpp:4576) / classify_team_strategy so Challengers orgs spread across DevelopmentFocus / TalentFarm / Rebuilding / BudgetRoster. TalentFarm scoring (potential-ovr upside, Team.cpp:849-851) is exactly the riser-feeder behavior wanted.
Give BudgetRoster a real risk_bias branch in auto_fill_roster's composite loop (Team.cpp:980-995) and a youth/upside term, so budget orgs aren't pure cheap-veteran buyers.

Add an in-roster role-rebalance step (not just count<5) so the D/C/I/S correctness invariant holds after the keep/drop pre-pass; today auto_fill only fires on vacancy (Team.cpp:1078, 1093). A 'wrong-comp full roster' is never corrected.

Decouple bench-depth gating from the 400K budget floor (Team.cpp:1822) for lower tiers, or add a tier-scaled developmental-reserve slot, so risers carry promotable depth. Pair with a potential/age floor in the FA sub-pool build (GameManager.cpp:4827-4834) to guarantee believable young talent is present.

Use the existing TierConfig struct (GameManager.cpp:4482) as the extension point: add per-tier knobs (strategy mix weights, min_potential floor, bench-allowed flag, salary realism) so T2 vs T3 behavior is data-driven and the promoted team (budget +800K at GameManager.cpp:4698) transitions believably into tier-1 economics.

Keep enforce_one_igl/enforce_one_flex (GameManager.cpp:4877-4878) and ovr_cap (4832) intact — they already deliver structural role/quality guarantees; the rebuild should layer differentiation on top, not replace these invariants.
